session.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Friday
session.description:
Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

session.title: Overnight TCG Freeplay - Friday Night
session.description:
Did you just get out of the Rave, or a late-night panel, perhaps? Are you looking to still have some fun at the Con overnight? Well, if you brought your own cards, we've got space! The TCG Room may close its outer doors at midnight, but we have a number of free-play tables available for BYO style play. Want to jam out some 2 AM Commander? We got you.

This area is not staffed between the hours of Midnight and 9 AM. All tables are available for use, but we ask that you please clean up after yourselves and behave responsibly in the game space. Failure to comply with this is a violation of intergalactic law. Thank you, and see you bright and early for more scheduled content!

session.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con – Grand Giveaway 2026
session.description:
Do you feel... lucky?

Come see if all those tickets you’ve accumulated from TCG events have won you greatness and glory… or just a giant stuffed Pokémon. It’s close enough to greatness, if you ask us.

session.title: Magic: the Gathering – Casual Commander w/ Loaner Library
session.description:
Come play a casual Commander game with friends, strangers, or even DRAW PHASE Staff!

Pods of 4 will be seated on-demand. No deck? No problem! We've got a library of pre-constructed decks you can borrow for the duration of your game. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

session.title: Cardfight Vanguard!! Learn to Play
session.description: Ever wanted to try out an Anime card game known for being easy to learn and incredibly fun to master? Cardfight Vanguard!! is right up your alley then! Come and play with one of our Starter Decks and be guided through a game by one of our volunteers! Complete a Learn to Play session and receive a ticket for the Sunday Giveaway!

session.title: Cardfight Vanguard!! - Free Starter Deck Showdown!
session.description: Ready for something a little bit more advanced? Did you just learn to play Cardfight Vanguard or are you a seasoned pro who just happened to see this event in your schedule app? (I see you). Either way, come on down and we'll furnish you with one of our starter decks, you'll play against some other Cardfighters and no matter what, you will get some cool prizes! Also for this year: You'll get to keep your deck! That's right; A free deck AND extra prizes! Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time. 8 Seat Event.

session.description: Di-Di-Digimon! Digital Monsters, The Card Game! Come help to save the digital world and learn to play this rich card game that has captivated audiences since 2020! Change yourself into a digital champion and save the digital world. And after you're done digivolving, you'll receive a pack on us to start your journey and a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

session.title: Echoes of Astra - Learn to Play
session.description:
This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

session.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
session.description:
Dear Con-goer, do you desire Flesh? Do you desire Blood? Perhaps even both at the same time? In that case, we at the TCG Room have you covered. Come see us and one of our Flesh Experts (Blood Experts available upon request) will teach you all the ins and outs of the Flesh and Blood TCG. Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

session.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Silver Age Precon Tournament
session.description: Step into the arena with FREE ready-to-play Silver Age preconstructed decks, officially sponsored by LSS. Experience the thrill of strategic 1v1 combat while making new friends in a fun and welcoming tournament setting! This event is a great chance to learn your first hero or try out a new class.All participants also receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

session.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Draft Night Party!
session.description:
Swing big for a knockout as cheers and boos amp up the volume in this Super Slam booster draft! Build a deck on the fly as a face (Revered) or heel (Reviled) to play in a limited environment that will 100% surprise you with its technical depth and intricacies. Brownie points if you watched that one SuperEyePatch Wolf video that uses anime to explain pro wrestling! Also we respect Brody King in this house.  All participants also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

BONUS: If you see a bald guy in a power-chair (Or just ask for Stein) and tell him your favorite wrestler, he'll give you an extra promo card! 

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

session.title: Grand Archive – Learn to Play
session.description:
Welcome to the Archive, Champion – do you have what it takes to forestall an apocalypse?

Come find out and learn Grand Archive, a growing new TCG fusing anime aesthetics with the best of western game design! Along with your demo kit, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

session.title: Grand Archive – Free Starter Deck Tournament
session.description:
You’ve learned how to start the fight, Champion – now let’s find out if you can finish it. Take up your provided starter deck, and go toe-to-toe with your peers in a test of skill alone. But remember: even in defeat, there is victory… in the form of a ticket to the Grand Giveaway.

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

session.title: HoloLive TCG - Learn to Play
session.description: Do you like VTubers? Do you like Trading Card Games? Have I got news for you. These two things are together at last and you, yes YOU can come and learn to play this brand new TCG with all your favorite HoloLive personalities. Just come over to the TCG Room and we'll have someone waiting to teach you! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday as well!

session.title: Shadowverse TCG - Learn to Play
session.description: Whether you've played the online TCG for Shadowverse or not, Shadowverse Evolve is a exciting and enjoyable game that promises to keep you on your toes constantly. Our resident Anime TCG Specialist will be here to teach you how to play and put you on your journey to becoming a champion. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

session.title: Weiss Schwarz - Learn to Play
session.description: Have you ever wanted every Anime ever made in one TCG? Well then you'll love Weiss Schwarz! Come relive climactic battles and moments from your favorite Anime and learn a mechanically deep TCG while you're at it! Hosted by our resident Anime TCG Expert, you'll be guided through your first game and then be on your way to being a champion! All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

session.title: Weiss Schwarz - Free Neo-Standard Tournament
session.description: Did you remember to bring your Weiss deck to con? If so, we think you'll love this Neo-Standard format tournament. Come on down to the TCG room and battle it out with other players to prove you're the best Weiss Schwarz player at Sakura-Con - and win some prizes, too! All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

session.title: Pokemon TCG – Learn to Play
session.description:
"Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."
Show more

session.title: Pokemon TCG – Free Build & Battle Event
session.description:
Oh, it’s all very fine and well to take your carefully-curated team to victory – but someone who’s true Champion material has that special something that lets them bond with a team they’ve only just met, intuitively understand their strengths and weaknesses, and lead them to glory.

How do you develop a talent like that, you ask?

Why, many rounds of Build & Battle, of course! Assemble your deck from the contents of the provided Build & Battle Box, and battle your way to to the top of the mountain… or at least to a good time. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

session.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Standard Tournament
session.description:
Welcome, Duelist! Here's something a bit more modern for you – a Standard Format Yu-Gi-Oh Tournament. Come over to the TCG Room and prove you're the best duelist at Sakura-Con (besides Kaiba, of course) and put your skills on display. Eager to show off your Ryzeal deck? Or maybe you're still playing Tenpai Dragon? – No matter what you're playing, we encourage you to stop by and win some prizes.

Three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Two loaner decks are available upon request (First come first serve) upon request as part of your reservation. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

session.title: Seibu Ryu Iai-Battōjutsu - Art of The Japanese Sword
session.description: Our presentation is a 1-hour demonstration of the Japanese martial art of Iai-Battōjutsu. In the presentation, we will give a history of the art while demonstrating a series of our techniques. We begin with basic draws of the sword and cuts. Next, we move on to kata (forms) demonstration, including a few basic, intermediate, and advanced kata. Finally, the last part of our demonstration is tameshigiri, or “test cutting” of tatami mats with live blades.In the past, we have also performed a choreographed sequenced and performed our tameshigiri at the closing ceremonies of SakuraCon, which we would be happy to do again.

session.title: Husky Wushu
session.description: Wushu is a Chinese martial art and international sport derived from hundreds of years of traditional Chinese Kung Fu. Husky Wushu has been an active club at UW since 2009, with a mission to foster an inclusive community around the practice of Chinese martial arts. We will be performing a medley of individual forms, group forms and fight sets, including hand forms and weapon forms such as sword, staff, and fan. We'll be doing a workshop after our performance, so stick around to learn some skills from us!

session.title: An Intro to Traditional Japanese Instruments with Dekoboko Taiko
session.description: Dekoboko Taiko invites you to learn about the rich history of traditional Japanese instruments, particularly percussion and winds, and their cultural influence in Japan and the world. This fun and interactive panel will include a performance and demonstration by members of the local Seattle group Dekoboko Taiko. Explore the festive beats of the taiko, the dancing clicks of the naruko, the lively melodies of the fue, and a few more surprises! Participants will be able to try out many of the featured instruments and enjoy a full performance from the group!

session.title: Diabolo (Chinese Yo-yo) Workshop
session.description: Apex Diabolo is a local performance group specializing in the diabolo, a traditional circus art and popular activity in Japan, Taiwan, and many other parts of the world. In this session, they will host a workshop to teach you the basics. Don't miss their special performance at the closing ceremony!

session.title: Samurai Undressed
session.description: Get up close and personal with the samurai!  Discover what lies beneath the armor figuratively and literally! See how the individual components of Japanese armor were worn and what types of accessories and weapons a samurai may have carried into battle. Learn about interesting places to visit in Japan if you’re a fan of these ancient warriors!

session.title: Steel sword fighting through Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA)
session.description: A showcase of Historical European Martial Arts, a study of various sword arts dating from 14th century all the way to 17th and 18th century. Realistically weighted steel training weapon simulators are used alongside protective gear to allow for as close to sword fighting in its purest form. Weapons include: longsword, rapier, sword & buckler, and saber.

session.title: Discover the Sound of Traditional Japanese Harps
session.description: Join our interactive panel presenting beautiful Japanese music!Koto no WA, ensemble of local koto (Japanese harp) musicians in the Greater Seattle Area, will play both traditional and contemporary songs, including some songs from anime series. This workshop will be a combination of live demonstrations, a brief history lesson on Japanese music culture, Q & A sessions, and a hands-on experience with the koto. Don’t miss this rare opportunity to experience Japan’s traditional string instrument with more than a thousand years of history.

session.title: Work in Japan on the JET Program: Info Session (Panel)
session.description: Are you interested in working in Japan? The Japan Exchange Teaching (JET) Program has brought over 80,000 participants from more than 80 countries to work in schools, boards of education, and government offices throughout Japan! The JET Program is the only teaching exchange program managed by the government of Japan. Learn more about the application process and hear from former participants about their time in Japan and the impact that the JET Program has had on their lives.Note: A bachelor’s degree is a minimum requirement for this job opportunity.

session.title: JET Program Alumni Meet-Up
session.description: “Once you’re a JET, you’re always a JET!” The Consulate-General of Japan in Seattle is teaming up with the PNW JET Alumni Association to host the JET Alumni Meet-Up! There is life beyond the BoE, and truth can be stranger than fiction when it comes to the daily life of a JET working in Japan! We know you’re here: come meet other nerdy alumni to connect, reminisce, and share your real-life anime-zing stories! If you're not a yet a JET, come join us to learn about the “JET Experience” from those who have “been there and done that” at this relaxed, fun, and informal gathering.

session.title: Sogetsu Ikebana Demonstration
session.description: Shroughout the world.  Sogetsu emphasizes individual creative expression using the materials of nature as the medium.  Megumi Schacher is a certified instructor with the rank of riji, which is the highest possible rank. She is an active member of the ikebana community, teaching, exhibiting, and demonstrating the art and practice of Sogetsu ikebana. Today she will create arrangements for you that introduce the concepts and techniques of ikebana.

session.title: Kyoto Animation Arson Attack - 7th Year Memorial Service
session.description: In July 2019, the studio and publisher Kyoto Animation experienced a horrific and devastating arson attack that resulted in the deaths of 36 young animators, making it one of Japan’s worst massacres in recent history. Because the 1st, 3rd, and 7th year memorial services are of particular importance in Japanese tradition, we feel that the people who lost their lives that day were not given the appropriate opportunity to be mourned by their fans due to the interruption caused by the COVID 19 pandemic.  We, as a local Buddhist temple that is a direct descendant of the 1,200 year old Japanese Shingon lineage, believe that being able to provide fans the opportunity to participate in a 7th year memorial service would be particularly significant. We feel that it is our obligation to provide the opportunity for people to grieve and mourn the lives of the victims and honor their memories. Through the Buddhist memorial service, we will be able to convey to the spirits of the deceased that their lives, works, and legacies are meaningful, and will be cherished. This will also help mourners attain the understanding that their lives are one with the victims of the attack. When a work of art moves someone, it becomes an inseparable part of them, and so we carry its legacy on with us through our own lives. In this way, we are all on a shared voyage, helping those people who have positively impacted our lives continue to live on through us, and we hope to allow fans of the works made by the fallen animators to experience a deeper connection with this understanding.

session.title: The Fabulous Furoshiki – Japanese Cloth Wrapping Techniques
session.description: Since ancient times, the Japanese have used a simple square cloth called a furoshiki to wrap and carry anything and everything. In today’s environmentally conscious age of maximizing use and minimizing waste, the furoshiki is making a strong comeback! Learn the basics of how to use this “eco-friendly” alternative to plastic bags and wrapping paper. This is a hands-on introduction to fun and functional Japanese furoshiki wrapping techniques!

session.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love
session.description: 2025 sucked. We all know it; it was basically the worst year ever. However, like any bold, tanned leader with blond wispy hair, our beloved Captain heard this claim and promptly responded: "Hold my Slurm®." Captain Zapp has thus established the "Department About Ruining Everything", and needs the worst, most poorly trained group of individuals imaginable to run it into the ground. Think you're the worst person for the job and will only make the world a worse place than it already is? Come in, apply, and if you're right, we'll give you the job on the spot!

Show more

session.title: Cosplay Wrestling Federation: Sakurumble VI! - Day 1
session.description: The CWF, THE MOST ELECTRIFYING show in Anime Sports Entertainment, returns to the Pacific Northwest at Sakura-Con to proudly present to you SAKURUMBLE as a two-day event! This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! Come join us to not only watch, but with your cheers and jeers steer the course of this exciting new installment of C! W! F!

session.title: Oriana Peron Drag Show Doors Open
Sakura-Con's Cosplay Lip Sync EXTRAVAGANZA!
session.description: Lip Sync Extravaganza is a family-friendly, all-ages event. Step into the spotlight and show off your best lip-syncing skills in this fun and dynamic event! Whether you’re channeling your favorite pop star, belting out a rock anthem, or grooving to an anime opening theme, the stage is yours to dazzle and entertain. Participants of all ages are welcome to showcase their talent and charisma, with songs suitable for all audiences. Bring your best moves, props, and enthusiasm to impress the crowd and our panel of judges.

session.title: Cosplay Chess After Dark - Mankind Vs. Monsters
session.description:
What happens when mankind and monster collide on a chessboard? What happens when they've given few rules, Kings with questionable motives, loads of innuendo, and dangerous props? 

You get this year's Cosplay Chess After Dark! Check out the dark, twisted, and salacious fights piloted by our Kings' unhinged desires.

session.title: Cosplay Chess ARENA! - FOR SCIENCE!
session.description:
From the ashes of many cheesecakes, inspiration has arisen! The mad minds of the Cosplay Chess team have concocted a truly devious experiment. A cosplay battle arena with the fights themselves being performed FOR SCIENCE!

Witness the latest iteration of cosplay combat and see the greatest What If our mad scientists could concoct.

session.title: The Floral Court Invitational: A Sakura-Con Nerd Prom
session.description:
Prepare for an evening where elegance blossoms and fandom flourishes! Sakura-Con proudly presents the Floral Court Invitational, an exclusive Nerd Prom event designed for those who dance to the beat of their own heart.

Step into a night of sophisticated celebration, where the vibrant spirit of anime and manga meets the timeless grace of a formal ball. Adorn your finest attire—whether it’s a stunning gown, a sharp suit, or the impeccably formal wear of your favorite character. This is your chance to shine, mingle with fellow enthusiasts, and create unforgettable memories under a canopy of cherry blossoms.

Be Crowned a Sakura Flower Messenger! The highlight of the evening is our prestigious Sakura Flower Messenger Contest. Aspiring envoys of elegance will showcase their unique style, grace, and passion for the Sakura-Con spirit. Judges will be seeking individuals who embody charm, poise, and a profound appreciation for our community. The chosen Flower Messengers will hold a place of honor, representing the beauty and camaraderie of Sakura-Con.

Indulge in an enchanting atmosphere, enjoy delightful music, and take to the dance floor as we celebrate our shared love for all things anime, manga, and Japanese culture. Join us for the Floral Court Invitational – where every dance is a story, and every attendee is part of a grand tradition!

session.title: Free Build Session
session.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

session.title: Mecha Modeling Contest
session.description:
The contest will have prizes across five categories:

1. Best Over All
2. Custom Build
3. Advanced Build
4. Straight Build
5. People’s Choice

While we do recommend pre-registering for the contest, on-site registration will also be available until 2 pm on Saturday, with judging and the awards ceremony taking place on Saturday at 6pm.

Pre-registration can be done using the form here :

https://forms.gle/Hp2gxn8mH3soNG3ZA

To participate in the contest, you will need to bring your kit to the Mecha Modeling room anytime on Friday or Saturday before 2pm. Only one entry is allowed per person. When registering, please choose the appropriate category for your kit. We reserve the right to move your kit to a different category if we feel it is necessary. People’s Choice voting will take place during the official judging so come to our room at 6pm on Saturday if you’d like to make your voice heard.

People’s Choice voting will take place during the official judging so come to our room at 6pm on Saturday if you’d like to make your voice heard.
Show more

session.title: Appleseed
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (1988 OVA - Subtitlted)

After World War III devastates the Earth, the utopian city of Olympus is governed largely by genetically engineered artificial humans known as Bioroids. When terrorists plot to use a powerful weapon called the “Holy Grail” to destabilize the fragile balance between humans and Bioroids, ESWAT officers Deunan Knute and the cyborg Briareos are drawn into a high-stakes conflict that threatens the future of the city.

session.title: Food Wars Episodes 1 - 4
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

Soma Yukihira dreams of surpassing his father as a chef in their family diner. When his father suddenly closes the restaurant and enrolls him in the elite Totsuki Culinary Academy, Soma must prove himself among the nation’s most talented young chefs. There, students settle rivalries through intense cooking duels known as shokugeki, turning every dish into a high-stakes battle.

session.title: Star Ocean Ex Episodes 1-3
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Subtitled)

Based on the video game series, the story follows Claude C. Kenni, a young officer aboard an advanced starship who is mysteriously transported to Expel, a medieval world of swords and sorcery. Mistaken for a legendary hero, Claude becomes entangled in a quest to save the planet from a looming catastrophe.

session.title: Project A-ko
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

When Earth faces a looming alien threat, the outrageously wealthy and arrogant B-ko is far more concerned with defeating her rival A-ko and winning the affection of their sweet, clueless friend C-ko. Armed with custom-built mecha and an army of loyal followers, B-ko wages over-the-top battles in a fast-paced parody packed with classic anime references, exaggerated superpowers, and a stylish red power suit.

session.title: Monthly Girls’ Nozaki-kun Episodes 1-4
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

High schooler Chiyo Sakura finally works up the courage to confess her feelings to her crush, Umetarou Nozaki—only to discover he’s a renowned shōjo manga artist who mistakes her confession for fan admiration. Instead of romance, she receives an autograph and an offer to become his assistant. Determined to get closer to him, Sakura steps into the chaotic, behind-the-scenes world of manga creation.

session.title: Is It Wrong to Try to Pick Up Girls in a Dungeon?  Episodes 1  - 4
session.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

In the bustling city of Orario, where gods and goddesses live among mortals and sponsor adventuring “familias,” 14-year-old Bell Cranel is the sole member of the Hestia Familia. Eager to prove himself in the deadly dungeon beneath the city, Bell’s life changes after a fateful encounter with the powerful “Sword Princess,” Ais Wallenstein—an encounter she’s unlikely to forget.

session.title: Cosplay Wrestling Federation: Sakurumble VI! - Day 2
session.description: The CWF, THE MOST ELECTRIFYING show in Anime Sports Entertainment, returns to the Pacific Northwest at Sakura-Con to proudly present to you SAKURUMBLE as a two-day event! This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! Come join us to not only watch, but with your cheers and jeers steer the course of this exciting new installment of C! W! F!

session.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love: Loveline!
session.description: Have you been catfished by an AI Chatbot? Do you have Love in Deep Space installed, or worse yet, tell people you do? Have you gone to a comedy relationship advice panel and taken what they said seriously? If you answered yes to any of these questions; then that's really embarrassing. Thankfully, we're here for you on a purely plutonic level! No question is too stupid, too lewd, too real or too fake, so bring us your hardest hitting and most debilitating relationship questions, and we will answer them for you, satisfaction guaranteed and sometimes in song, or your money back!

session.title: AMV History 2000+ and Fundamentals
session.description:
Presented by Chris Allen and Cheri Allen

Join us on a fast‑paced ride through the evolution of AMVs! Starting from the law-breaking chaos of the early 2000s to today’s high‑power, pro‑level creations. We will unpack the tech, tricks, and cultural turning points that shaped the AMV community. Whether you’re a first‑time editor or a seasoned master, you'll learn sharp insights on storytelling, audio, visual quality, and the tools that make modern AMVs shine.

session.description:
Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Have you ever seen a gut-busting AMV and wanted to make one just like it? Comedy AMVs can be some of the most memorable, diverse, and uplifting videos in any contest, and they can be either very simple or very complex! This panel will use several videos as case studies to showcase the many ways to brainstorm, edit, and fine-tune a comedy AMV. By the end, you’ll know how to squeeze every giggle out of your audience!

session.title: A Study of Tatsuki Fujimoto: The Man Behind Chainsaw Man
session.description: Following the release of 2022's Chainsaw Man, and the hit 2025 Reze Arc film, Tatsuki Fujimoto has quickly made a name for himself as one of the premier creators of the manga industry. With his bold, cinematic visual style, and gripping, eccentric storytelling, it is rare to find a creator that captures the hearts of millions of fans all over the world writing seemingly whatever he wants! If you are interested in exploring the underlying themes of works such as Chainsaw Man or Fire Punch, or even just learning some funny anecdotes from this largely mysterious figure, then join in for some lively discussion!

session.title: Melodies of life (Final Fantasy Music Trivia)
session.description: Few gaming franchises have shaped the soundscape of the industry quite like Final Fantasy. From the joyful rhythm of a Chocobo ride to the triumphant swell of the Victory Fanfare, the series’ iconic themes have become an emotional soundtrack for generations of fans. Crafted by legendary composers such as Nobuo Uematsu, Yoko Shimomura, and many more, these pieces are woven into the very identity of the franchise—its true Melodies of Life. In this interactive panel, attendees will put their musical memory to the test. Join us as contestants listen, guess, and compete to see whether the timeless music of Final Fantasy still resonates in the hearts of its most devoted fans. Whether you’re a seasoned Warrior of Light or a newcomer drawn by nostalgia, come celebrate the magic, history, and harmony of one of gaming’s greatest legacies.

session.title: Ghost in the Shell and the Stray Dog of Anime Mamoru Oshii
session.description: One of the most seminal anime directors to have ever lived Mamoru Oshii, has had an indelible mark on pop culture as a whole. While most famous in the United States for the 1995 Sci-Fi classic Ghost in the Shell, Oshii has created a number of other works covering ideas just as nuanced and thought provoking, with tones ranging from the melancholic to the absolutely absurd! If you are interested in exploring the underlying ideas of Ghost in the Shell, and finding other works that could possibly scratch that same itch, then join in as we discuss the career of Mamoru Oshii, a man always questioning whether our reality could really be true.

session.title: Spicy Pokemon Jeopardy
session.description: Hey guys, did you know that a Gardevoir is coming to you tonight with a brutally spicy Jeopardy game? Do iconic Pokemon like Vaporeon, Lucario, and Lopunny make you feel a certain way? In this fast-paced barrage of cultured Pokemon content, teams of enlightened adults compete against each other and the entire audience for prizes. This Python-based Jeopardy game that some random Gardevoir cosplayer coded from scratch will feature thighs, grippers, memes, and other stimulating categories. Regardless of previous experience with the Pokemon franchise, attendee participation is crucial in each of the two rounds that will be played! Come, cringe, and laugh while indulging in the chaos flowing through the screen! Warning: After this panel, you may be able to 1) identify Pokemon based on conspicuous features, 2) perceive certain Pokemon in ways you never have before, and 3) more fully enjoy your convention experience.

session.title: The Gojo Effect
session.description: Have you ever wondered why people are obsessed with Satoru Gojo from the series Jujutsu Kaisen? Do you, yourself, love him? Do you hate him and want to know what it is about him that has hypnotized our community? Well, join us as we explore what about him has everyone captivated and how he has impacted the history of anime/manga and society going forward.

session.title: Disability and Access in Witch Hat Atelier
session.description: In the world of magic, you are either born a witch or you aren't. This is supposedly to keep people from misusing magic to harm others, but Coco and company continue to see the inequity and suffering caused and emphasized by lack of access and judgment of ability. In this panel, we'll explore how Witch Hat Atelier's whimsical story is a clear commentary on issues we experience in our world on a daily basis, and why those who need access the most are often barred from it.

session.title: Avatar: Seven Havens - What We Know So Far
session.description: 20 years after the debut of Avatar: The Last Airbender, co-creators Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko are expanding their beloved franchise again in 2027 with another 2D animated series: Avatar: Seven Havens. Set years after The Legend of Korra, both the physical and spiritual worlds have been devastated by a cataclysm, and the Avatar is feared as humanity’s destroyer rather than its savior. The premise signals a bold new direction for the franchise. Can Avatar: Seven Havens live up to the standards of what many consider one of the greatest animated series ever, or will it face the same divisive reception as Korra, Netflix’s 2024 adaptation, and a certain 2010 film best left unnamed? Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside fellow local fans for an interactive discussion and Q&A on what Avatar fans truly deserve—and whether we’re actually going to get it.

session.title: Um, Actually: Con Edition

Based on the DropOut TV show, contestants will be tasked with correcting pedantically incorrect for the chance to win points. Whoever has the most points by the end of each round will win, and claim a prize. There will be three rounds, each of which is themed based on the kinds of statements need corrected (e.g. Western Media, Japanese Media, Gaming, etc.) And the best part yet? Audience members are encouraged to participate! So come and join us!

session.title: Pokemania: A Look Back at When Pokemon Took the World by Storm
session.description: Move over Mickey, there's a new mouse in town! When Pokemon was first released in 1996, few people realized just how big it would become. Over the next few years, it took the world by storm and started a Pokemon obsession that would lead to the most profitable media franchise in the world. Whether you're hoping to relive those glory days or wishing to get a glimpse into a time when you weren't even born yet, join us as we look back at the Pokemon craze of 1999 in North America.

session.title: Transitioning Humanity: This Is Not A Man
session.description: What do Arcane, Creature Commandos, Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, and The Witch from Mercury have in common? All four explore the ethics and effects of human augmentation – of transhumanism. They ask how far science and technology can take humanity, and whether the cost is worth it. Join us for an in-depth examination of these new transhumanist narratives, how (if) they’ve been updated for our modern age, and where these shows draw the line on humanity.

session.title: Opening Notes: Analyzing the Musical Heart of Anime
session.description: Ever wondered about the musical structure of anime openings? Join us as we explore the musical complexities behind iconic openings like Attack on Titan and Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End. We’ll break down the harmony, form, and techniques that make these openings unforgettable. No prior music knowledge is needed—we’ll make it accessible for all attendees.

session.title: Danmei 101 & Trivia!
session.description: An interactive panel about the wide world of Chinese BL, where after learning some fun facts, audience members can try their luck at some jeopardy questions! A panel perfect for beginners and fun for experts, there's something for everyone here! From Heaven Official's Blessing to more obscure picks like White Cat's Divine Scratching Post, there's sure to be fun for even the grumpiest of cultivators.

session.title: Dungeons & Disappointment: Stop Rescheduling, Start Playing
session.description: Cancelled another game session? Added another half-finished campaign notebook to the stack? We say no more! Come join us and identify keys for building a strong TTRPG table from both the GM and player perspectives. Learn tips to run and play in consistent, efficient, and engaging games. Receive resources for supporting scheduling, pushing plot, and crafting memorable (and complete) campaigns. Systems highlighted: D&D, Kids on Bikes, Obojima, Path Finder, and Call of Cthulhu.

session.description: Fandom can mean something different for everyone. Some find friends and family. Others find heated fan wars and a plethora of trolls. What does Fandom mean to you? Let's debate on whether Fandom are toxic or genuinely good.

session.title: FanDUMBS: A Fair & Balanced Analysis
session.description: We all can agree that fandom is cursed. All fandom. Any fandom. Yes, even your fandom. But why is that? What makes fandom the worst possible thing that a person can be invested in besides NFTs? Learn more as your completely normal and not-at-all-cringey hosts guide you through the history of bad fandom, regrets, and MAYBE how we can prevent this from happening to future fandoms.

session.title: Oh! That's A Baseball Episode!!
session.description: Have you ever just been watching an anime when suddenly, out of left field, all the characters start playing baseball for an episode and it's the greatest thing ever? Whether it's there to throw a curveball into how you see the characters, or a change-up into the pacing, or just to bring out their animation aces, this is a panel about how anime's baseball episodes really hit it out of the park.

session.title: ElfQuest: An American Manga
session.description: For almost 50 years, ElfQuest has straddled the line of what defines an American comic and what defines manga. A look at the series, and why it could be considered an American manga.

session.title: Anime Glasses Mega(ne) Panel!
session.description: Glasses are really versatile! Come see just how much anime can do with glasses as a prop and character design tool, with plenty of examples of funky frames and spectacularly bespectacled specimens. We've got the anime glasses gleam, we've got cyber glasses, we've got glasses with windshield wipers on them, we've got nerds and gentlemen, teachers and spies! The potential of glasses is truly terrifying.

session.title: Fifth Gen Isn’t Pokemon
session.description: Generation five of pokemon introduced a lot of new concepts to the series like ripping off their previous aesthetic designs, min-maxing stats to optimize the removal of actual pokemon from competitive play, and whatever Garbador is. When it comes down to it the abominations that were introduced in Pokemon Black and White are nowhere near as majestic as classic pokemon like Muk, Mr. Mime, and Electrode.

session.title: How To Cosplay Chess - Creating Chaotic Cosplay Content
session.description: What do you get when you cross live theater, improv, and a bunch of cosplaying nerds? You get Cosplay Chess! If you've ever been curious about the event or have never heard of it before today, this is the panel for you! Join our panelists as we discuss how 50+ people get organized into 2 hilarious games of Sakura-Con Cosplay Chess! Learn how the selection process works, how easy it is to apply, and the many different roles required to make Cosplay Chess happen! Get answers to questions like, "How do you choose who makes it into Cosplay Chess? What are Stage Ninjas? Can I be [CHARACTER NAME] next year?" And more!

session.title: Mecha History and Cultural Impact: Now on Blu-Ray!
session.description: From the beginning of modern anime, there have been robots. But where did the word "robot" come from? When did they become giant, and where did the word Mecha come from? Just how giant can they get, and how powerful? What should I watch if I'm curious, but totally unfamiliar? What is Tokusatsu, and how does it relate to Power Rangers? If you've ever had any of these questions, this is a good place to get them answered! The history goes back farther than you would believe, and the impact on culture spreads farther than you would expect!

session.title: Umamusume Horse-Story - Legends on the Track
session.description: Whether you're just making your debut in the Uma world or if you're already a top trainer, saddle up for a trip through Umamusume history! In this panel, you'll learn the real life stories of Japan's most iconic racehorses. Behind every Umamusume is a horse racing legend - together, we'll race through the triumphs and heartbreaks of their careers, and learn how they shaped the personalities of our favorite trainees. Every race tells a story - and we can't wait to share them!

session.title: Cultural Cross Polination
session.description: What's the most obscure reference you've ever seen in a cartoon, or anime, or a TV show? Remember when South Park had a full episode about Pokemon? Or when FLCL had a few seconds done in South Park's style? That's what this panel is all about. References, homages, parodies, back and forth, east to west, west to east. It goes back further than you'd think, and in many cases recently, it comes full circle where homages are being referenced, and made into whole new references!

session.title: Kanto Pokemon Food Web
session.description: Have you ever been curious about what Pokémon eat out in the wild? Well look no further than this presentation. Learn about what a food web created using the first generation of Pokémon would look like. It shows which Pokémon might prey on each other and how their size, typing and inspiration might affect that. It also tells us which species might be major producers, apex predators, or keystone species. Additionally we will be discussing what exactly a food web is, how it differs from a food chain, how Pokémon were categorized in order to put them onto the food web and how environmental factors change which Pokémon are included.

session.title: The 5 W's of Sumo - A Factual (But Fun!) Guide to the Basics
session.description: Sumo is having a resurgence of popularity in Japan, thanks to new and powerful young wrestlers, but getting into the sport can be confusing. Who are all of these people? What are they doing? What are those poses?! Was that a FACE SLAP? Is that allowed??? Where are their clothes?!?! Why was this even invented? Let's run through the basics of this religious-ceremony-turned-national-sport to answer all of these questions and more!

session.title: Is That An Isekai? You As An Attendee Can Cast A Vote!
session.description: Join this panelist & co. by voting on a variety of both Eastern and Western shows/movies/books/etc. as to whether you think they count as Isekai or not! This will be a casual panel with some back-and-forth by the two panelists and a majority vote from the audience for each concept. Every time a vote is taken, the winning result will be recorded. This panel will be held at multiple other conventions in the US over the next few years in order to get data on how regional differences may affect these opinions.

session.title: Moto Hagio: Reality, Gender and Identity
session.description: Legendary writer and manga artist, Moto Hagio, called the "God of Shoujo Manga", has written some of the most influential stories across multiple genres but is scarcely known in popular media in the United States. This panel will discuss, in brief, some of the ground-breaking themes in her books and series that have contributed to and shaped many Japanese manga artists that followed after.

session.title: Examining the Cultural Impact of One Piece
session.description: Let's set sail for adventure and dive deep into analyzing the impacts of the cultural phenomenon that is One Piece. From its impact on media to the intersection of manga and real world politics, join the FWHS Anime/Manga Club to discuss why this formula has won out over a crowded field of Shonen manga and anime to reshape the medium.

session.title: Symbology Of Japanese Textiles 2.0
session.description: Every stitch tells a story. In The Symbology of Japanese Textiles 2.0, we’ll explore the meanings woven into traditional kimono and obi. These symbols aren’t just decorative; in anime, character outfits often use traditional patterns to signal personality traits, backstories, and hidden narrative clues. Join me as we uncover how textile symbolism enriches Japanese culture and storytelling, adding depth, identity, and intention to everyday garments and beloved characters alike.

session.title: Japanese Urban Legends 2.0
session.description: Japanese urban legends are like wasabi: they’ll make your eyes water, leave you a little shaky, and you’ll still come back for more. From eerie phone calls from the dead to bathroom ghosts who love asking awkward questions, these tales have haunted generations. And the best part? A couple of these legends aren’t just stories — you can actually go visit the real-world spots where they’re said to lurk. Pack your courage (and maybe some extra tissues), and let’s dive in.

session.title: Naoya Nakayama Director's Panel Rhythm, Motion, and Emotion
session.description:
Join us for the visual mastery behind YOASOBI’s “Idol” music video, and live performance direction on UMA MUSUME PRETTY DERBY: NEW ERA'S DOOR, dance animation for KAGUYA-SAMA: LOVE IS WAR, and ending sequences for OSHI NO KO!
Naoya is especially skilled at condensing story and emotional intensity into short runtimes, and is highly regarded as a creator who balances pop appeal with visual beauty. He is currently active across multiple fields, including music videos, ending animations, and live performance direction.
In this panel, Nakayama will discuss his creative process, approach to character performance, how he designs animation around music and timing, and what goes into directing short-form and music-centered animation. From pop spectacle to delicate acting moments, discover how he compresses story and energy into every frame.

session.description: Meet a useless Goddess, a pink haired boy, a sexy genius fox girl, and a tiny mischievous yordle creature with a fairy all at once! Faye Mata has voiced over 100 credited characters in video games and animated series, worked as a games industry program manager, an eSports competitor, and a wholesome content creator, and you can ask her anything!

session.title: Sakura-Con and Me: My First Step into Anime Conventions with Hiroshi Nagahama
session.description: Hiroshi Nagahama grew up inspired by American comic book heroes and dreamed of visiting the U.S. Sakura-Con made that dream a reality. He looks back on his first trip to Seattle in 2002, the excitement of his first anime convention, and how meeting fans in America influenced his creative work.

session.title: Crunchyroll Presents: Witch Hat Atelier Premiere Screening
session.description: Everyone knows that magic-users are born, not made. This being a hard truth to swallow, Coco is about to give up her lifelong dream to be a witch ... until she meets the mysterious magician, Qifrey. By secretly watching Qifrey perform magic, Coco realizes that maybe what seemed like the truth is not exactly reality. Her “magical” dream may not be as far away as it seems… Join us for the premiere screening of Witch Hat Atelier.

session.title: Snowball Earth – Episodes 1 & 2 Special Screening
session.description:
The earth has frozen over—and a new story of survival begins.
Join us for a special screening of Episode 1 and the Global Premiere of Episode 2 of the TV anime Snowball Earth!

Based on the manga by Yuhiro Tsujitsugu, Snowball Earth depicts a breathtaking, ice-covered future Earth brought to life through stunning animation and a powerful narrative. This special panel offers fans the rare opportunity to experience the very beginning of the series ahead of its global streaming.

Don’t miss this exclusive chance to witness the birth of Snowball Earth and receive a giveaway!

Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

87604.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Friday
87604.description:
Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

87143.title: Embracing Cringe: Enjoying Media As Autistic
87143.description: "Cringe"—the terrifying buzzword that criticizes a certain way of enjoying media and fandom that shares a lot of overlap with autistic traits. At this panel, we will examine the inherently ableist nature of "cringe" and why media/fandom are common comforts for those with autism.

Show more

86766.title: Examining the Cultural Impact of One Piece
86766.description: Let's set sail for adventure and dive deep into analyzing the impacts of the cultural phenomenon that is One Piece. From its impact on media to the intersection of manga and real world politics, join the FWHS Anime/Manga Club to discuss why this formula has won out over a crowded field of Shonen manga and anime to reshape the medium.

87093.title: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle! Integrating Sustainability with Cosplay
87093.description: Did you know textiles are the fastest growing type of waste in the country? This panel will give an overview of the problems surrounding textile waste by a recycling industry expert and explore how we can use creative approaches to incorporate sustainability into cosplay and crafting.

86925.description: Thinking about starting a cosplay group—or joining one—but not sure where to begin? This panel covers the basics of creating and running a cosplay group, from finding members and choosing themes to communication, planning meetups, and handling group dynamics and more.

86513.title: Sonic: An Unfortunate History
86513.description: Sonic the Hedgehog is a staple holding together the childhood of any proper 90s kid, but just like Soap shoes he’s tragically out of fashion. We’ll be going over the history of Sonic The Hedgehog games, Sega Consoles, Crush 40, and various chili dogs.

86790.title: When Anime Takes the Stage
86790.description: What happens when our favorites leave the 2D world to enter our 3D space? We get the wonderful world of 2.5D. We’ll examine the origins of 2.5D and the many aspects that bring our favorite stories to the stage! This panel focuses specifically on adaptations for anime, manga, and video games for the stage. From the stage design to original songs and casting there’s so much to explore!

85682.title: Free Build Session
85682.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

86387.title: AMV History 2000+ and Fundamentals
86387.description:
Presented by Chris Allen and Cheri Allen

Join us on a fast‑paced ride through the evolution of AMVs! Starting from the law-breaking chaos of the early 2000s to today’s high‑power, pro‑level creations. We will unpack the tech, tricks, and cultural turning points that shaped the AMV community. Whether you’re a first‑time editor or a seasoned master, you'll learn sharp insights on storytelling, audio, visual quality, and the tools that make modern AMVs shine.

87793.description:
"When the longshanks of Isger start to gather troops, the goblins of the Chitterwood Forest get nervous. As an ally of the Chitterwood goblins, the Crookedtoes tribe has requested that the Pathfinder Society offer some protection during the discussions, making sure that the only fires and explosions that occur are from goblin hands!
---
Game Host: Mike
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87799.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
87799.description:
Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

87810.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
87810.description:
Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

86549.title: Plurality in Anime and Real Life
86549.description: The notion of multiple people sharing a body has always been intriguing to storytellers, but many people aren't aware that plurality isn't fictional! Japanese media in has depicted plurality in various forms, so we'll be taking a look at what's accurate, what's not, and what we love anyway.

87744.title: Eyes on the Prize: Catseye Crown
87744.description:
"Eyes on the Prize is a collaborative game of court intrigue and fake dating, in which players assume the role of characters in a couple pretending to be very much in love in order to climb the social ladder of the court for personal gain: be that wealth, status, their families approval, or whatever other motive. Players enact a series of scenes to develop their characters, the relationship -however it turns, and to advance their goals. 

For this session, the backdrop of the drama is the courtiers, some legitimate, some less so, vying to be chosen as the successors to, or position themselves in advantageous relationship to, the hairless Catseye Queen. 

Will your character find wealth, power, friendship, failure, embarrassment, or even true love of one kind or another? Let's find out!
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87731.description:
"Come join the creator of the Shadow Scar TTRPG for a night of fun and festivity as you enter the Hollow Eye Masquerade! Important people from across the multiverse have been gathered together for this once in a lifetime soiree thrown by the head of the Hollow Eye Crime Syndicate, Malacoda. As ninja agents of the secretive Shadow Scar Agency you've been sent to infiltrate the party and find out what the Hollow Eye Syndicate is planning with all these important people. While you're there you'll also have to keep your eyes peeled. Intelligence suggests that you won't be the only curious party. The monstrous yokai might be sending their own agents to kidnap the guests or worse.

"Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

86975.title: Level Up Your Cosplay Sewing!
86975.description: Are you a beginner to intermediate cosplay sewist wondering how to improve and polish the quality of your stitched cosplays? Come and learn easy tips and tricks to level up your projects with various cutting/marking, stitching, pressing, and finishing techniques.

87095.title: Talking About Writing Talking: How to Improve Your Dialogue
87095.description: "Writing dialogue is the worst!" she said, flopping down into her chair. "I can never get it to sound natural. Why do characters have to talk to each other so much!?" Does this sound like you? You're not alone! Join us for a discussion about writing dialogue - what to think about, how to find character voice, what to cut and how to use dialogue to effectively convey information and move your plot and character relationships forward.

86515.title: The Fall of the House of Miyazaki
86515.description: Hayao Miyazaki did a lot of work to make anime acceptable to mainstream audiences. Not by creating high quality art, but by making something so bad that everything else looked quality in comparison.
86515.description_display: Hayao Miyazaki did a lot of work to make anime acceptable to mainstream audiences. Not by creating high quality art, but by making something so bad that everything else looked quality in comparison.

86927.description: Have you been meaning to start learning Chinese? Have you ever wondered how to say "Shizun!" while reading your favorite danmei? Ever wanted to talk about your favorite characters, but didn't know how to say their names out loud? Come on by and learn the basics of Mandarin pronunciation and aid your danmei journey!

87050.title: Fic Binding: Bringing fanfiction from screen to page through bookbinding
87050.description: We love fanfiction. We love books. So why not turn fanfiction into books? Learn all about the idea of and how to take your favorite fanfictions and bind them into proper books that you can actually hold in your names and show off in your bookcases. From one-shot quick binds to multi-chaptered hardbound novels, let’s get started.

86768.title: Symbology Of Japanese Textiles 2.0
86768.description: Every stitch tells a story. In The Symbology of Japanese Textiles 2.0, we’ll explore the meanings woven into traditional kimono and obi. These symbols aren’t just decorative; in anime, character outfits often use traditional patterns to signal personality traits, backstories, and hidden narrative clues. Join me as we uncover how textile symbolism enriches Japanese culture and storytelling, adding depth, identity, and intention to everyday garments and beloved characters alike.

86347.title: Voice Acting Secrets with Cassandra Lee Morris
86347.description: Cassandra Lee Morris has voiced over 200 roles in video games, anime and animation. How'd she get there? What's the secret behind breaking into voice acting? How the heck does she create all those voices? Cassandra answers your questions and more in this one-on-one panel.
86347.description_display:
Cassandra Lee Morris has voiced over 200 roles in video games, anime and animation. How'd she get there? What's the secret behind breaking into voice acting? How the heck does she create all those voices? Cassandra answers your questions and more in this one-on-one panel.
Show more

87145.title: Code-Switching No Jutsu: Navigating Fandom Spaces While Black
87145.description: Therapist AC Mateen knows what it’s like to love anime and have to shift your whole vibe depending on the room you’re in. This panel unpacks what it means to be Black in fandom... the pride, the exhaustion, the inside jokes that save us. We’ll talk about why we shrink ourselves sometimes, how that shows up even in our cosplay choices, and how to reclaim our joy without apology. Expect laughs, honesty, and maybe a little group healing (the anime kind, not the awkward trust-fall kind).

85654.title: Cosplay Wrestling Federation: Sakurumble VI! - Day 1
85654.description: The CWF, THE MOST ELECTRIFYING show in Anime Sports Entertainment, returns to the Pacific Northwest at Sakura-Con to proudly present to you SAKURUMBLE as a two-day event! This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! Come join us to not only watch, but with your cheers and jeers steer the course of this exciting new installment of C! W! F!

87858.title: Maid: The Roleplaying Game: Luck Be a Meido Tonight: Casino Chaos!
87858.description:
Total maidness awaits as you try to make your master's casino grand opening a winner, even as bizarre random events throw everything into chaos.

It's grand opening night, the culmination of your master's fondest wish: a casino themed after, and wholly staffed by, maids just like you. And they're betting it all that you'll be the crew that makes it a resounding winner! And if you can hit the jackpot and win the master's favor for your performance? Then you'll be the one to join them at the inaugural all-you-can-eat buffet, catered by your fellow maids.

Alas, lady luck's decided to leave everything up to chance. Fire in the kitchen? Long-forgotten ghosts? Love dodecahedrons? A heist run by aliens? A time vortex leading to a tragic past? Surprise costume upgrades? And come to think of it, isn't the master themself even an embodiment of random chance?
---
Game Host: Saoirse
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ (vulgar humor, sexual references, slapstick violence)

86160.title: Naoya Nakayama Director's Panel Rhythm, Motion, and Emotion
86160.description:
Join us for the visual mastery behind YOASOBI’s “Idol” music video, and live performance direction on UMA MUSUME PRETTY DERBY: NEW ERA'S DOOR, dance animation for KAGUYA-SAMA: LOVE IS WAR, and ending sequences for OSHI NO KO!
Naoya is especially skilled at condensing story and emotional intensity into short runtimes, and is highly regarded as a creator who balances pop appeal with visual beauty. He is currently active across multiple fields, including music videos, ending animations, and live performance direction.
In this panel, Nakayama will discuss his creative process, approach to character performance, how he designs animation around music and timing, and what goes into directing short-form and music-centered animation. From pop spectacle to delicate acting moments, discover how he compresses story and energy into every frame.

85683.title: Free Build Session
85683.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87753.title: D&D 5e (2024): Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn
87753.description:
"Uni the unicorn is in trouble. The evil spellcaster Kelek has stolen Uni’s horn to power a fell ritual. If the characters don’t stop Kelek, Uni’s horn will be destroyed forever in Kelek’s quest for power.

Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn is part of a yearlong celebration of Dungeons & Dragons and its 50th anniversary. The adventure presented herein involves characters from the beloved 1980s Dungeons & Dragons animated series.

This adventure is designed for four to six level 4 characters. Accompanying the adventure are character sheets for six of the animated series’ protagonists: Bobby, Diana, Eric, Hank, Presto, and Sheila, all presented in young adulthood. The seventh character sheet presents Niko, a Cleric from a different set of adventurers who recently tumbled into the D&D multiverse. Each player can choose one of these characters or provide a character of their own.
---
Game Host: Joseph 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

86587.title: Um, Actually: Con Edition
86587.description: Nerds like a lot of things, but above all else, they love correcting people! This is Um, Actually! Do you know a lot of nerdy facts about media and nerd culture? Do you want to test your nerdy knowledge for the chance to win a prize? Then come join us for another round of Um, Actually! Based on the DropOut TV show, contestants will be tasked with correcting pedantically incorrect for the chance to win points. Whoever has the most points by the end of each round will win, and claim a prize. There will be three rounds, each of which is themed based on the kinds of statements need corrected (e.g. Western Media, Japanese Media, Gaming, etc.) And the best part yet? Audience members are encouraged to participate! So come and join us!

86977.title: Forged in Plastic: Flexible 3D Printing and the Art of Cosplay Printing
86977.description: Ever wondered how cosplayers and makers create armor that moves with them — not against them? Join Ann Grace Hub, a nonprofit makerspace in Bremerton, WA, for a deep dive into flexible 3D printing and how modern materials are reshaping what’s possible in costume design and functional fabrication. This session walks through the process of designing and printing scale armor and wearable pieces using flexible filaments like TPU and hybrid blends. We’ll cover the fundamentals — from printer settings and pattern slicing to assembly methods and heat-forming — and share insights learned from teaching these techniques in our community classes. Whether you’re a cosplayer, engineer, or curious maker, you’ll see how affordable printers and open-source models can help you build armor that bends, breathes, and battles con floors with style. Presented by Ann Grace Hub, a  nonprofit dedicated to empowering creativity through access to fabrication tools and education.

87628.description:

87786.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 1: Scrambling the Tribes [PFS2 7-09]
87786.description:
"When the longshanks of Isger start to gather troops, the goblins of the Chitterwood Forest get nervous. As an ally of the Chitterwood goblins, the Crookedtoes tribe has requested that the Pathfinder Society offer some protection during the discussions, making sure that the only fires and explosions that occur are from goblin hands!
---
Game Host: Steven S
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87794.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 2: The Battle of Logas [PFS2 7-12]
87794.description:
"With the success of the Chitterwood Talks, the goblin tribes are ready to march on Logas and put a stop to the undead attacks, with support from the Pathfinder Society. As forces approach the city, rumors start to circle of a strange plague only affecting Hellknights, leaving the undead Hellknights covered in bizarre, circular scars and bearing the mark of an unknown order. Within Logas, a Hellknight necromancer has declared herself the head of a new Hellknight order and is using the city as her chance to prove its worth. Can the Pathfinders stand up to The Order of the Open Tomb?

He’d also love to hear your thoughts and impressions on the works he’s been involved with.

86389.description: With so many AMV contests out there with floods of entries, it can be turbulent and sometimes even confusing to navigate the high seas of submission rules. Come on board and join our crew of AMV editors turned AMV Contest Coordinators as they provide tips and techniques to help steer you in the right direction. Oh, and questions are always welcome!

87147.title: The Good Fan Etiquette Guide To Recommending Anime
87147.description: The Good Fan Etiquette Guide To Recommending Anime is the must-see panel for any anime fan who needs a comedic how-to guide on how to recommend anime to their friends, family, loved ones, and even strangers they meet out in the world. From explaining the patented VAULTELF system of recommendation consideration to applying it to real-world examples. This guide is your one-stop shop to navigating the fraught world of recommending anime to those new to the genre.

87733.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
87733.description:
"You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87099.title: Cracking Open a Cold One With The Catboys | Your Guide to Fishing in Final Fantasy 14
87099.description: Fellow Warriors of Light, you've traveled to far off lands, fought foes threatening the very fabric of this star, striving endlessly for ever greater heights. Perhaps you seek a moment of respite, a quiet, peaceful opportunity to take it slow and drink in the sights. Just you, your rod, the crisp summer morning air, the sun cresting over the horizon, an alarm clock going off at 4 AM, an unreasonable number of spreadsheets, and a deep seated feeling of torment and despair. You stare into the deep inky waters. You see nothing but an abyss. You see a reflection of yourself. Fishing is not for the faint of heart, but together we can chart a course through these treacherous and terrible waters. Welcome to the True Endgame. Fishing has Raidplans too. This panel will not contain story spoilers, but will contain references to locations visited through the most recent expansion, Dawntrail. --- BORN TO FISH WORLD IS A LAKE Reel Em All 2025 I am FSH main 410,757,864,530 CAUGHT FISH

85684.title: Free Build Session
85684.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87189.title: Intellectual Property for Creators
87189.description: This panel provides a comprehensive introduction to Intellectual Property (IP) for creators, including artists, writers, designers, and freelancers seeking to pick up the basics to support the creative industry. Explore the fundamentals of copyrights, trademarks, and other IP rights to understand how they protect your work. Learn practical strategies for safeguarding your creative assets, addressing infringement, navigating licensing, and fair use. Gain the knowledge and tools needed to protect your rights and ensure your creative work remains secure.

85720.title: Steel sword fighting through Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA)
85720.description: A showcase of Historical European Martial Arts, a study of various sword arts dating from 14th century all the way to 17th and 18th century. Realistically weighted steel training weapon simulators are used alongside protective gear to allow for as close to sword fighting in its purest form. Weapons include: longsword, rapier, sword & buckler, and saber.

87717.title: Call of Cthulhu : Poison Soup (Doku-iri Sūpu)
87717.description:
"After going to sleep in your bed, you awake in the night to an unnerving scene. You find your self in a square room surrounded by strangers and in the center is a lone desk and chair. On the desk sits a bowl of bright red soup and a note that reads, ""If you want to leave, you have one hour to drink the poison soup, or you will be collected..."" Will you escape with your lives? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG, Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided. 
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

Learn how they combine their professional life with their side-gigs or hobbies, how those two sometimes intersect, and leave inspired to use your creativity across all aspects of your life!
