87123.title: Let's Talk About Major Character Death
87123.description: There's no easy way to say it—death is an uncomfortable and deeply personal subject. A lot of people understandably avoid it. So why does fandom still have a small but dedicated population of writers telling stories where our favorite characters die, our ships are separated, and funerals are held? This craft panel seeks to create space for discussing the special role fandom and fanfiction can play in talking about grief and loss, how Major Character Death (MCD) is different in fanfic and original fiction, as well as how and why fanfic writers explore these themes in our work.

87721.title: Call of Cthulhu: The Son of Nyx (Nyukusu No Musuko)
87721.description:
"The year is 2098 and you are crew on the spaceship Sakigake conducting a mineral survey on the surface of Pluto. But when you begin your journey back to earth, disaster strikes. A red-black swirling celestial body crashes into Pluto causing untold destruction. The last thing you see before losing consciousness are the eyes of an unspeakable cosmic horror rising from the chaos. Can you escape your doom and return to earth? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided.
---

This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! 87835.title: Pokemon Scarlet & Violet Tournament (Switch)
87835.description: Team Scyther's Swiss Doubles Tournament! Take part in a Pokemon doubles tournament using VGC standard rules (Regulation F). Bring your Switch and copy of Scarlet or Violet to participate! We will offer prizes to the top two spots and there will be door prizes! Detailed description of the format Using the most recent Pokemon games and switch console - must be provided by contestants to enter. Pokémon Restrictions: Available Regional Pokédex. Pokémon Limits: Team of 4 to 6 Pokémon from Level 1 to Level 100. All Pokémon are then set to Level 50.Time: 10 Minutes Team Preview: 90 Seconds Turn Time: 60 Seconds We are aiming to have side events and options for digital engagement in the Panel space, too. In previous years, we have had turnouts of 20-40 and have managed with smaller spaces, but we'd prefer to have enough space to have opponents facing each other at tables.

Detailed description of the format Using the most recent Pokemon games and switch console - must be provided by contestants to enter. Pokémon Restrictions: Available Regional Pokédex. Pokémon Limits: Team of 4 to 6 Pokémon from Level 1 to Level 100. All Pokémon are then set to Level 50.Time: 10 Minutes Team Preview: 90 Seconds Turn Time: 60 Seconds We are aiming to have side events and options for digital engagement in the Panel space, too. In previous years, we have had turnouts of 20-40 and have managed with smaller spaces, but we'd prefer to have enough space to have opponents facing each other at tables.
Show more

87790.title: Pathfinder Society: Lacking Respect [PFS2 Q23]
87790.description:
"A Pathfinder Society Quest designed for 1st- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
Not everyone in Irrisen has been happy with the rule of Queen Anastasia. A group of bandits found it much easy to sate their violent tendencies during the rule of the prior queen. Now they're causing trouble both in Irrisen and in settlements on its western border. It's up to the PCs to put a stop to their banditry.
---
Game Host: Nick H
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87798.title: Starfinder Society: The Beasts of Bo: Part Two [SFS2 1-16]
87798.description:
"A Starfinder Society Scenario designed for 3rd- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
The Starfinder Society’s negotiations with Bo-Shek and the Beasts of Bo have been interrupted by a violent joint assault launched by agents of the Functionary and forces from the Wreck of the Returned. Amid chaos and explosions, the Starfinders on site team up with the Beasts of Bo to take down this greater threat and avoid their mutual destruction. Will you survive the assault? 

This is an adventure in the ongoing Invasion’s Edge metaplot, and is part two of a two-part scenario series. This adventure is a direct sequel to Starfinder Society Scenario #1-14: The Beasts of Bo: Part One.
---

Session duration: 3 hours

87663.title: Pokemon TCG – Learn to Play
87663.description:
"Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

87756.title: Old Gods of Appalachia: Best Leave Them Ghosts Alone
87756.description:
"Attending the funeral of a childhood friend is never an easy thing, but when it’s the funeral of someone you accidentally killed and then brought back to life years ago, well . . . you all have owes, and this one’s about to come due. Best Leave Them Ghosts Alone is an Old Gods of Appalachia horror adventure set in a small town full of memories, ghosts, and promises unpaid. This poignant adventure, rife with atmosphere, horror, and emotion, was an official Monte Cook Games event at Gen Con 2023, and is particularly well suited for use as a unique standalone experience.

The Old Gods of Appalachia Roleplaying Game is a horror game, and as such, is full of things that might disturb your dreams, follow in your footsteps, and haunt your waking hours. This adventure includes elements such child death, grief, funerals, ghosts, and hauntings.
---
Game Host: Juli
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+ Due to horror theme

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87165.description: Calling all Phantom Thieves! There will be a panel about the heartstealing JRPG known as Persona 5. A few Phantom Thieves will conduct the panel and we will be discussing about treasures, corrupt hearts, and maybe calling cards to bring forth a change of heart. if you must infiltrate Mementos or any Palaces as well as taking your time doing social activities during this panel, That's all good, This is a meeting for any Phantom Thieves that wish to attend! Warning: This panel may discuss topics such as abuse, self-harm, and other sensitive topics that Persona 5 depicts, Attendee discretion is advised.

87739.description:
"Help your friend get a date with their crush at Adventuring Camp! We will do a bit of mad-libs style of improv to create the love interest and the romantic rival as a group. The players will decide who is going to be the pursuer, and everyone else will be their wing-person in this adventure. Get your friend a date before camp ends! We will use pre-generated characters for this adventure that follow character trope archetypes. No DnD experience needed!

87034.title: A Decade of Osomatsu-san: A Fan Retrospective
87034.description: Ten years ago, Osomatsu-san brought gag manga characters from the 1960s into the modern age with a bombastic reboot. Since then we’ve had four anime seasons, movies, games, and oh so much merchandise featuring the iconic identical sextuplets. How has Osomatsu’s success changed the landscape of anime, and how has Osomatsu changed? Whether you can't tell the Matsunos apart or you're a die-hard Karamatsu Girl, come join us!

85775.title: Inside GeekGirlCon
85775.description: GGC is an annual convention that takes place in fall every year that celebrates the contributions of under-represented communities in comics, gaming, STEM, sci-fi/fantasy, fandom, and more! 86951.title: Doomed Yaoi Tournament: How doomed are they?
86951.description: Doomed yaoi? We hardly know her! Or do we? With BL on the rise, we bring you a bracket-style tournament that includes audience debate participation and voting. We aim to answer the ultimate question: Who is the most doomed yaoi pairing? Players will volunteer to argue on behalf of a doomed yaoi ship on the bracket (if no volunteers, then panelists will step in, don't worry), and then the audience will vote on who they feel is more doomed.

85692.title: Free Build Session
85692.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

86754.title: I Watched the Lowest-Rated BL Animes: Bigger and Longer

I'm here to tell you if they were misjudged, or if they deserve to be purged from the face of the earth. Join us for the drama, the insanity, the genuinely sweet and the genuinely traumatizing moments. (Extended Edition)

87213.title: Battles With and Without Honor: Exploring Yakuza in Film
87213.description: Family and betrayal. Hope and oppression. Gun and sword. Join us as we explore the history of Japanese gangs across the country’s cinema, from the roots of the genre in honorable samurai films to the explosive action films of modern times. We’ll touch on a variety of topics—such as the influences of Japan’s post-war prosperity and class struggle, major directors such as Kinji Fukasaku and Takashi Miike, and a slew of must-watch yakuza films, both the violent and the contemplative. Then stick around for an open audience discussion on the yakuza genre—whether you want to share your own facts or just talk about your favorite movies with others, you’re welcome to join!

86681.title: Fifth Gen Isn’t Pokemon
86681.description: Generation five of pokemon introduced a lot of new concepts to the series like ripping off their previous aesthetic designs, min-maxing stats to optimize the removal of actual pokemon from competitive play, and whatever Garbador is. When it comes down to it the abominations that were introduced in Pokemon Black and White are nowhere near as majestic as classic pokemon like Muk, Mr. Mime, and Electrode.

86748.title: Is That An Isekai? You As An Attendee Can Cast A Vote!
86748.description: Join this panelist & co. by voting on a variety of both Eastern and Western shows/movies/books/etc. as to whether you think they count as Isekai or not! This will be a casual panel with some back-and-forth by the two panelists and a majority vote from the audience for each concept. Every time a vote is taken, the winning result will be recorded. This panel will be held at multiple other conventions in the US over the next few years in order to get data on how regional differences may affect these opinions.

86615.title: Pokemania: A Look Back at When Pokemon Took the World by Storm
86615.description: Move over Mickey, there's a new mouse in town! When Pokemon was first released in 1996, few people realized just how big it would become. Over the next few years, it took the world by storm and started a Pokemon obsession that would lead to the most profitable media franchise in the world. Whether you're hoping to relive those glory days or wishing to get a glimpse into a time when you weren't even born yet, join us as we look back at the Pokemon craze of 1999 in North America.

87736.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
87736.description:
"You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

85698.title: Mecha Modeling Contest
85698.description:
The contest will have prizes across five categories:

1. Best Over All
2. Custom Build
3. Advanced Build
4. Straight Build
5. People’s Choice

While we do recommend pre-registering for the contest, on-site registration will also be available until 2 pm on Saturday, with judging and the awards ceremony taking place on Saturday at 6pm.

Pre-registration can be done using the form here :

https://forms.gle/Hp2gxn8mH3soNG3ZA

To participate in the contest, you will need to bring your kit to the Mecha Modeling room anytime on Friday or Saturday before 2pm. Only one entry is allowed per person. When registering, please choose the appropriate category for your kit. We reserve the right to move your kit to a different category if we feel it is necessary. People’s Choice voting will take place during the official judging so come to our room at 6pm on Saturday if you’d like to make your voice heard.

87631.title: Echoes of Astra - Learn to Play
87631.description:
This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

87032.title: Inuyasha and Trauma Informed Care
87032.description: For the second year in a row, come learn about the principles of trauma informed care through the lens of one of your favorite characters, Inuyasha! We'll discuss how Inuyasha's trauma has informed his behavior and connect trauma informed care to the positive interactions that lead to his character development. Open to all, healthcare providers and those interested in learning about caring for loved ones with trauma.

87261.title: Seattle Cosplay Resources (On a Budget!)
87261.description: This panel is designed to help cosplayers on a budget achieve their prop and costume dreams. As someone who makes cosplay out of a lot of recycled materials, I wanted to share the resources in Seattle I've found that have helped with that. This panel will also include local fabric/material stores that have relatively cheap options compared to options online. After Joanne's closed down, I think it's important to spread awareness of the small businesses our community has easily accessible to us. Cosplay is for everyone!

86643.description: If a panel about the wonders of BL sounds even remotely up your alley, then have we got the chance encounter for you! This is the perfect opportunity to hear about BL from an enthusiast of the genre, and why it's perfect for all kinds of readers and viewers. This lecture-style talk about BL is perfect for the open mind, and who knows, maybe there's a thing or two to be learned!

87600.title: Monthly Girls’ Nozaki-kun Episodes 1-4
87600.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

High schooler Chiyo Sakura finally works up the courage to confess her feelings to her crush, Umetarou Nozaki—only to discover he’s a renowned shōjo manga artist who mistakes her confession for fan admiration. Instead of romance, she receives an autograph and an offer to become his assistant. Determined to get closer to him, Sakura steps into the chaotic, behind-the-scenes world of manga creation.

87762.title: Break!!RPG: A Completely Normal Adventure
87762.description:
"The peaceful domain of Millbrook faces its darkest hour. A monstrous beast stalks the woods, leaving destruction in its wake. The just Lord Vance grieves for his kidnapped daughter, Princess Helena, taken from her very tower. His scheming Vizier whispers plots in shadowed halls while a mad street prophet warns of apocalyptic doom. Anarchists stir in the taverns, an orphan child claims mythic power, and a traveling merchant dealing in secrets watches it all unfold with knowing eyes. 

You've been called to save the princess, slay the beast, and restore order to this troubled land. It should be simple, every hero knows this story. But as you peel back the layers of Millbrook's tragedy, you'll find things are not as they seem. 

Even monsters, they say, can love.

Expect twists, moral complexity, and the chance to either prevent-or survive-a spectacular disaster.
---
Game Host: Mia
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87752.title: Exalted Essence: Spiritual Warfare
87752.description:
"You and your allies are working to demonstrate the illegitimacy of the opressive Realm by undermining their divine mandate. Use your social-fu and sorcerous might to talk,  cajole, seduce (pg-13), bind, and ensnare the gods and spirits of the Blessed Isle to rebel. 

This will be more role play and social combat heavy rather than physical combat. 

Choose from premade characters including subversive anarchist punk rock kings of hell, shapeshifting infiltrators with useful backers, to inheritors of the divine god kings making amends for their past lives' sins.

CW structural oppression and systems of coercive control. 
---

87215.title: The Alfee: The Best Japanese Band You've Never Heard Of
87215.description: With 51 years of continuous activity, 59 consecutive Top 10 singles, and nearly 3000 concerts, The Alfee is Japan’s premier arena rock band. We’ll talk about their contributions to anime and tokusatsu music, along with their killer vocal harmonies, Biblically-accurate guitars, the time bandleader Toshihiko Takamizawa played Ultraman in a short, and much more.

With 51 years of continuous activity, 59 consecutive Top 10 singles, and nearly 3000 concerts, The Alfee is Japan’s premier arena rock band. We’ll talk about their contributions to anime and tokusatsu music, along with their killer vocal harmonies, Biblically-accurate guitars, the time bandleader Toshihiko Takamizawa played Ultraman in a short, and much more.
Show more

87127.title: Anime Murder Mystery
87127.description: A body has been discovered! There has been a murder at Sakura-Con and it is up to you to help in the criminal investigation. Question suspects and find the culprit in this thrilling, interactive anime-themed mystery presented by Anime Discovery Project at UW.
87127.description_display:
A body has been discovered! There has been a murder at Sakura-Con and it is up to you to help in the criminal investigation. Question suspects and find the culprit in this thrilling, interactive anime-themed mystery presented by Anime Discovery Project at UW.

87077.title: Intro to Vinyl Anime Dolls
87077.description: A presentation on vinyl anime dolls like Dollfie Dream, Obitsu, and Smartdoll. We will cover brands, doll care, and how to customize your doll. We will demonstrate aspects such as pose-ability and customization. This panel is SC-13 but contains sensitive content as vinyl dolls are intended for a 15+ audience. The dolls and panel are not intended for young children.

85728.title: JET Program Alumni Meet-Up
85728.description: “Once you’re a JET, you’re always a JET!” The Consulate-General of Japan in Seattle is teaming up with the PNW JET Alumni Association to host the JET Alumni Meet-Up! There is life beyond the BoE, and truth can be stranger than fiction when it comes to the daily life of a JET working in Japan! We know you’re here: come meet other nerdy alumni to connect, reminisce, and share your real-life anime-zing stories! If you're not a yet a JET, come join us to learn about the “JET Experience” from those who have “been there and done that” at this relaxed, fun, and informal gathering.

86361.title: Faye Mata AMA
86361.description: Meet a useless Goddess, a pink haired boy, a sexy genius fox girl, and a tiny mischievous yordle creature with a fairy all at once! Faye Mata has voiced over 100 credited characters in video games and animated series, worked as a games industry program manager, an eSports competitor, and a wholesome content creator, and you can ask her anything!

86571.title: Anime so bad it's good ... the second course
86571.description: We all know about great anime, wonderfully crafted stories, and breathtaking performances. But on the other end of the spectrum is camp! Media that is so bad, it’s good. Join us O_O ...as we serve up a brand new menu of some of the campiest and schlockiest anime.

87743.title: Daggerheart: Rising Through the Fell Graves
87743.description:
"Finally, on this rainy, dreary night, you and your party have almost caught up to Dr. Sincipital, an infamous and prolific necromancer. The only thing standing in your way is a labyrinth of a graveyard and the feeling that something is tethered to your spirit. Fight, claw, and blast your way through undead to reach the man you're hunting with the Daggerheart system! 
---
Game Host: Eden
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87687.title: Magic: the Gathering – Free Curated Chaos Draft
87687.description:
Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

87730.title: Ryuutama: Eat Your Veggies Before They Eat You – A Delicious In Dungeon Inspired Adventure
87730.description:
"Calling all dungeon delvers with an appetite for adventure! Rumor abounds of a mythical, monstrous vegetable that grants amazing strength and magical powers. You and your party are tasked with acquiring this legendary vegetable while eating your way through the dungeon one monster at a time. Cook up your kills and see how it effects your skills. Will you create a stat-boosting feast, or flop with status-effecting slop?

Learn to play Ryuutama, described as “Hayao Miyazaki’s Oregon Trail,” during this homebrewed adventure with level 2 characters. Bring your imagination and let's get cooking!
---
Game Host: Camille
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

86509.title: Learn Para Para!
86509.description: Learn a short Para Para dance routine with gyaru! Para Para is a Japanese synchronized dance style performed to Eurobeat music. It became popular among gyaru in the 90's and early 2000's! Para Para mainly involves side stepping and arm movements, so it's perfect for both very beginner dancers, and is fun for those more experienced as well!

86537.title: Spicy Pokemon Jeopardy
86537.description: Hey guys, did you know that a Gardevoir is coming to you tonight with a brutally spicy Jeopardy game? Do iconic Pokemon like Vaporeon, Lucario, and Lopunny make you feel a certain way? In this fast-paced barrage of cultured Pokemon content, teams of enlightened adults compete against each other and the entire audience for prizes. This Python-based Jeopardy game that some random Gardevoir cosplayer coded from scratch will feature thighs, grippers, memes, and other stimulating categories. Regardless of previous experience with the Pokemon franchise, attendee participation is crucial in each of the two rounds that will be played! Come, cringe, and laugh while indulging in the chaos flowing through the screen! Warning: After this panel, you may be able to 1) identify Pokemon based on conspicuous features, 2) perceive certain Pokemon in ways you never have before, and 3) more fully enjoy your convention experience.

87030.title: The Realities of Found Family: Adoption in Japan
87030.description: From SPY×FAMILY to Ace Attorney, there are many heartwarming and dramatic portrayals of families formed by means besides genetic lineage. How does real life compare though? What facets of adoption don't make it into your favorite anime or JRPG? What differences are there between adoption in the USA and adoption in Japan? If you're interested in learning more, you can come listen to me yap about all this.

87715.title: Daggerheart: Witch of the Meadowbrook Bog
87715.description:
"There is a distraught parent in the tiny town of Meadowsbrook, asking for assistance to have their lost infant returned.  With the challenge of political vying between the mayor Montgomery and the witch Agatha, duplicitous dealings from community members, and no right answer, the party will be thrust into a situation that challenges their morals, ethics, and notions.  This session will include topics of natalism, memory manipulation, and small community dynamics.  Character sheets and ability cards provided!
---

Age rating: Teen (13+)

87778.title: Monster of the Week : The Curse of Whispering Pines
87778.description:
"Love dark modern fantasy? Love wacky punk antics? Then you'll love Monster of the Week! Each party memeber picks one role to form their team of radical monster hunters!

""The small town of Whispering Pines, WV was once a picturesque site along winding state roads where city folks would drive up to take photos of the fall colors as the dense forest trees turned in the autumn. In recent years, however, strange disappearances have left the otherwise-peaceful town worried that someone dangerous is on the loose."" 
---

Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ I like to get a tad edgy :3

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87804.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
87804.description:
Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

87815.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
87815.description:
Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

86645.title: Multiverse Comedy Show
86645.description: The world, nay the universe needs to laugh! Using the Improbability Drive to out-maneuver the Vogon fleet; we’ve gathered personalities from across the galaxies for a brand new show! Introducing "Multiverse Got Talent", a cosplay comedy show that unites fandoms to prove that humor brings us together. Using the Improbability drive; your host, Steve Katz, will be your guide for the evening, as you journey across the bridge of the Enterprise; or the swamps of Far Far Away; or into the TARDIS itself. No matter who pops in, you will leave wanting more. Remember, Don’t Panic… cause its time to laugh! (Simply put: comedians in cosplay performing in character; and it’s a ton of fun!)

86784.title: Cosplay Figure Drawing
86784.description: Need inspiration for your next piece? Want to reference your favorite character irl? Just want to practice fun poses? Come to our figure drawing studio! The sessions will consist of several rounds of incrementally timed sets with different poses and models in cosplay. Come with your art tools of choice, or pencils and paper will be provided We will have models of different body types and from multiple fandoms. All models will be clothed in accordance with Sakuracon dress code and policies.

85013.description: Unleash your inner superstar in this high-energy lip sync battle for adults only! Whether you’re channeling your favorite diva, rocking out to a power ballad, or bringing the heat with a sultry performance, this is your chance to take the stage and captivate the crowd. With no limits on creativity (or a bit of sass), participants will battle it out to claim the title of Best In Show in this electrifying 18+ Lip Sync Battle.

86685.description: Anime Survivor takes your favorite anime characters and strands them on an island where they have to compete until only one is left. We utilize a web-based simulator to find out once and for all which anime character has what it takes to be the sole survivor.

85693.title: Free Build Session
85693.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87697.title: Riftbound - Learn to Play
87697.description:
Riftbound! A TCG based off of the world of League of Legends, but this time in physical card form! A brand new game from UVS Games & Riot Games, Riftbound provides League of Legends gameplay without the searing rage of going 0/8/3 on Summoner's Rift. Unfortunately when your opponent defeats you however, you will not be able to brush it off as 'support diff'. On the other hand, if you win you experience the joy that can only be obtained from getting 22 kills as Garen while the enemy top laner prays for the surrender vote to succeed.

All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

Disclaimer: Riftbound is very new, extremely popular, and impossible to get product for, so unfortunately we will not have anything extra to send you home with, sorry!

Swing big for a knockout as cheers and boos amp up the volume in this Super Slam booster draft! Build a deck on the fly as a face (Revered) or heel (Reviled) to play in a limited environment that will 100% surprise you with its technical depth and intricacies. 86955.title: Beyond Ito: A Horror Anime and Manga Iceberg
86955.description: This panel takes audiences on a guided descent through the horror anime and manga iceberg, beginning with familiar titles and plunging into the genre’s most obscure and unsettling depths. Hosted by the Seattle Scare Society, the discussion explores how horror evolves across anime and manga while highlighting hidden gems and extreme themes. Attendees can also expect interactive trivia throughout the panel, with chances to win prizes as they test their knowledge and journey deeper into the iceberg together.

86725.title: Cute or Criminal: Romance? In Anime
86725.description: Whoop whoop! That's the sound of the police! They're coming for you because there are some things in anime that are just down right illegal. Join us for a spin off of our long time panel, Cute or Creepy: Romance in Anime, where we will explore the more mature side of romance in anime. This is an 18+ panel that relies heavily on audience participation, so be sure to bring your wit and old-timey detective hat, as we decide whether clips are cute or criminal.

86820.title: The Dying Art of Whump
86820.description: It goes by many names—whump, hurt/comfort, hurt/no comfort, major character injury, and more. Whether it's an arrow to the knee or something more serious, fanfic writers like hurting their favorite characters. This craft panel looks at the fine art of whump as a form of storytelling nearly unique to fanfiction, its history, popular tropes, and asks a central question: is the genre endangered?

87036.title: Race to the "Top”!
87036.description: Ever looked at a character in a totally-not-yaoi anime and thought, “Yeah… he’s definitely a seme”? Well, tonight is your night. Join us for the ultimate bracket battle where eight “Seme” contenders and eight “Uke” icons from across anime go head-to-head — and you help decide who claims the crown. Expect chaotic debates, unhinged logic, passionate shipping energy, and possibly several friendships being questioned. As the bracket narrows, we’ll reach the Final Showdown where the winning Seme and Uke face one last fate-defining question: Do they ship? Audience participation is highly encouraged. Judgment will be loud. Logic will be questionable. And yes, we’re talking about that kind of power dynamic.

87764.title: Nachatte Murder Mystery: A Pseudo Murder Mystery : A Snowed-In Tragedy
87764.description:
"In the remote, snow-covered mountains, a secluded cabin offers warmth and refuge to weary travelers. A place to drink, laugh, and escape the bitter cold. But not all who enter seek shelter. Last night was supposed to be a night of celebration, but when you wake with a pounding headache and a hazy memory, you realize something is terribly wrong. A body lies cold in the living room, and none of you can recall what happened. Was it an accident? A crime of passion? Or something far more sinister? With the storm raging outside and no way to leave, there’s only one way forward; piece together the events of last night before the truth is buried beneath the snow forever.
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87131.title: Moon Prism Power, Deconstruct!: The Magical Girl Genre Examined
87131.description: A beautiful transformation sequence, a cute animal familiar, and plenty of villains of the week – is that all you need for a magical girl anime? What is the real core of the genre, beyond selling toys to children? Join us as we take an in-depth look at the development of magical girl anime, from early works like Princess Knight to genre-definers like Sailor Moon to supposed subversions like Madoka Magica. Together, we'll tease out those true magical girl themes and transform our understanding of the genre.

87133.title: ERP Dos and Don'ts
87133.description: Do you struggle with writing? So do I! Making interesting characters is such a challenge, and don't even think about putting them in a situation until you have at least a PhD in conflict. There has to be a better way to refine your skills as a writer. Now there is! Today we'll be delving into the intricacies of writing Fanfic, roleplaying our favorites, and creating original content! To do this, we'll be taking a cue from painters who have long used nude models to best understand the human form. That's right; we're talking about smut and what it can do for you.

86959.title: The Music of Visual Kei
86959.description: Visual kei is an artistic movement gaining fresh attention from younger generations thanks to social media, but it’s much more than just fashion. Music lies at the heart of visual kei’s identity. Join us for a fun listening session, where we’ll explore the music of classic and modern visual kei bands, and maybe you’ll discover your next favorite artist!

87514.title: Yuri on Ice: Totally a Sports Anime… Right?
87514.description: It’s been nearly a decade since Yuri on Ice broke hearts, boundaries, and genre expectations. Hailed as a BL gateway for many, it also captivated fans who never expected to care about figure skating—or its alleged romantic subtext. This panel is for the “Yuri-curious,” the skeptical, heteronormative-leaning, and the secretly converted. We’ll explore how Yuri on Ice blends sports, romance, and emotional storytelling to complicate these paradigms, and perhaps “manly” genres have always been a little softer on the inside. Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside a Q&A panel with anime fans who can even provide deeper insight.

87009.title: Lucifer's Lounge: CLUB 666 - Hellaverse Dance Party
87009.description: Back by highly popular demand, Lucifer’s Lounge is back, going bigger, louder, and deeper into the 7 rings with CLUB 666, a full-scale Hellaverse dance party experience for fans of Hazbin Hotel and Helluva Boss! TO CLEAR UP CONFUSION WE RECEIVED LAST YEAR: This event is a nightclub-style DJ type dance party. It is NOT a choreographed dance panel. Attendees are encouraged to move freely, vibe, and enjoy the music in a high-energy club atmosphere. DJ SETS: Hyu Yoon - 45 minutes SCR4TCHK4T - 45 minutes Music will include official tracks, fan-favorite songs, and remixes from across the Hellaverse fandom, including material from Hazbin Hotel Season 2 and the newly released Helluva Boss studio soundtrack, along with other songs mixed in to keep the energy up! Cosplay is strongly encouraged, kandi trading is welcome, and audience participation is entirely free form. So if you're down to party like in Beelzebub's hive, JOIN US IN HELL! ❤️ ⚠️ This event is intended to feel like a club environment and will include loud music and/or flashing lights.

87601.title: Is It Wrong to Try to Pick Up Girls in a Dungeon?  Episodes 1  - 4
87601.description:
ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

In the bustling city of Orario, where gods and goddesses live among mortals and sponsor adventuring “familias,” 14-year-old Bell Cranel is the sole member of the Hestia Familia. Eager to prove himself in the deadly dungeon beneath the city, Bell’s life changes after a fateful encounter with the powerful “Sword Princess,” Ais Wallenstein—an encounter she’s unlikely to forget.

87608.title: Overnight TCG Freeplay - Saturday Night
87608.description:
Did you just get out of the Rave, or a late-night panel, perhaps? Are you looking to still have some fun at the Con overnight? Well, if you brought your own cards, we've got space! The TCG Room may close its outer doors at midnight, but we have a number of free-play tables available for BYO style play. Want to jam out some 2 AM Commander? We got you.

This area is not staffed between the hours of Midnight and 9 AM. All tables are available for use, but we ask that you please clean up after yourselves and behave responsibly in the game space. Failure to comply with this is a violation of intergalactic law. Thank you, and see you bright and early for more scheduled content!

85694.title: Free Build Session
85694.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87606.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Sunday
87606.description:
Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

87011.title: How to Plan, Prepare and Maximize your Time Enjoying Japan (in 17 easy steps)
87011.description: If you are planning to go to Japan, this is the panel for you. I will cover a multitude of topics including flights, hotels, immigration process, transportation, how to attend festivals, ticketed events, how to navigate the metro system and more. I am not going to tell you what the best plan is to go to Japan, rather I will give you the information and tools that you will need to create the best plan for you. If you are going to attend only one Japan travel panel this year, make it this one… we have prizes too!

86401.title: AMV 201: Advanced Techniques
86401.description:
Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Take your AMVs to the next level! If After Effects plugins leave you puzzled, you're struggling to break into finals, or you just want some cool new techniques to add to your toolkit, then you've come to the right place. We'll answer your questions and help troubleshoot common problems. We'll show off the tools and processes that have helped us the most. Whether you're trying to impress a YouTube audience or a Sakura-Con judge, this panel is a must for all aspiring AMV editors!

87649.title: Grand Archive – Free Constructed Tournament
87649.description:
You’ve learned how to start the fight, Champion – now let’s find out if you can finish it. Take up your provided starter deck, and go toe-to-toe with your peers in a test of skill alone. But remember: even in defeat, there is victory… in the form of a ticket to the Grand Giveaway.

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

86649.title: Opening Notes: Analyzing the Musical Heart of Anime
86649.description: Ever wondered about the musical structure of anime openings? Join us as we explore the musical complexities behind iconic openings like Attack on Titan and Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End. We’ll break down the harmony, form, and techniques that make these openings unforgettable. No prior music knowledge is needed—we’ll make it accessible for all attendees.

86687.description: From fabric choices to techniques- how do you make your cosplay feel rooted in reality? When does a cosplay call for fine silks vs. simple cottons. 86611.title: Defying Fate: A Higurashi Panel
86611.description: Higurashi: When They Cry is a series that takes great joy in redefining itself at every turn. You think you have it figured out, but you were only looking at a fragment of the whole picture. For newcomers: let’s dig in to how to approach this unique work of art. For veterans: let’s talk about what makes it so special, what keeps it in our hearts even 20 years later.

86575.title: Law for Creatives and Weebs and Creative Weebs
86575.description: When do you need a lawyer? What do lawyers look for in contracts? What is a copyright and how do you get one? Is Phoenix Wright accurate? (Not legal advice!)

87175.title: Let’s Talk About The Ranma ½ Reboot
87175.description: In 2025, Ranma ½ received the reboot treatment—an updated take on a globally beloved series known for blending shonen and shojo storytelling. Long praised for its playful exploration of gender expression and fluidity (literally involving water of varying temperatures), Ranma ½ remains an influential martial arts rom-com for many lifelong anime fans. Does the reboot honor its source material, or does it fall short? How might it realign with the original 1980s manga? This panel is for Ranma ½ fans ready to dive deep into the lore, debate its legacy, and at least agree on one universal truth: Ryouga has the worst sense of direction. Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside fellow local fans for an interactive discussion and Q&A.

87791.title: Starfinder Society: Mystery of the Frozen Moon [SFS2 1-02]
87791.description:
"A Starfinder Society Scenario designed for 1st- through 2nd-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
Known to the Starfinders only as Vast-723-Arctic-Moon, or VAM-723 for short, Luvoth is one of four small moons in orbit around an unexplored gas giant in the Vast. Initial scans of the moon indicate there’s no sapient inhabitants, but there are some artificial structures—an intriguing mystery! Believing VAM-723 could be the perfect jumping off point to explore the local stellar bodies and surrounding system, the Starfinder Society dispatches a team of agents to perform initial surveys of the moon and investigate the structures.
---
Game Host: Steven S
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Starfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

87816.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
87816.description:
Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

87716.title: Blades in the Dark: Cache Grab
87716.description:
"You scoundrels have been approached by a broker in the sunless city of Doskvol, offering lucrative coin for running a score.  The job is to steal as much as you can from a weapons cache in the district of Coalridge, known for its abandoned railcars and derelict coal mine.  Players will be set into a session of warring factions, labour rights, and a resistance movement that threatens not just the Akorosi Empire, but the common man.
Character sheets and dice provided!  
---
Game Host: Æther K (they/xe)
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87719.title: Call of Cthulhu : Poison Soup (Doku-iri Sūpu)
87719.description:
"After going to sleep in your bed, you awake in the night to an unnerving scene. You find your self in a square room surrounded by strangers and in the center is a lone desk and chair. On the desk sits a bowl of bright red soup and a note that reads, ""If you want to leave, you have one hour to drink the poison soup, or you will be collected..."" Will you escape with your lives? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG, Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided. 
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87780.title: Old School Essentials Advanced: The Grotesques Grotto
87780.description:
"Three weeks ago an earthquake cracked open the Babbling Grotto, an outlawed temple of Iakos, wild god of secrets and prophecy. Now the cave “speaks”: ghost-chants, impossible laughter, and visions that feel too true.

Three days ago, royal witch hunters entered to stamp out the heresy…and never returned. In their place, something called the Grotesques keeps the temple’s prisoners, steals memories, and puppets the dead with runic stones.

You’ve been hired to go in, bring the hunters back (or their bodies), and haul out whatever relics survived a millennium of forbidden worship. But the grotto doesn’t just kill intruders—sometimes it remembers you differently when you try to leave

---
Game Host: Steve Bulla
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87040.title: Analyzing anime using Erick Erickson’s Psychosocial Stages of Development
87040.description: Ever wonder why you like you favorite anime? Well in this panel we will talk about some ways that people connect with anime through the eyes of psychosocial stages. We will also discuss "objectively" what anime is best ;) Come for a good time and hopefully learn something.

87013.title: Failing Isn’t a Bad Feeling—It’s a Superpower
87013.description: In today’s world of social media and instant gratification, failure is often seen as something to avoid at all costs. We hate it, ignore it, and sometimes even fear it. But here’s the truth: I’ve failed more times than I can count—and I’ve learned to love it. Failure isn’t the end; it’s the beginning of growth. Every setback teaches resilience, creativity, and perseverance. My biggest inspiration? Anime characters who fail constantly yet keep pushing forward. They stumble, fall, and face impossible odds—but they never quit. That mindset changed everything for me. Failure means you’re trying. It means you’re learning. And in a world obsessed with perfection, embracing failure might just be your greatest strength.
87013.description_display:
In today’s world of social media and instant gratification, failure is often seen as something to avoid at all costs. We hate it, ignore it, and sometimes even fear it. But here’s the truth: I’ve failed more times than I can count—and I’ve learned to love it. Failure isn’t the end; it’s the beginning of growth. Every setback teaches resilience, creativity, and perseverance. My biggest inspiration? Anime characters who fail constantly yet keep pushing forward. They stumble, fall, and face impossible odds—but they never quit. That mindset changed everything for me. Failure means you’re trying. It means you’re learning. And in a world obsessed with perfection, embracing failure might just be your greatest strength.
Show more

87860.title: Maid: The Roleplaying Game: At the Masquerade of Maidness!
87860.description:
"Join your fellow maids at an abandoned estate, cleaning it up for a night of revelry, dark secrets, and jealousy  as you dance to the tune of a long-dead master.

You all arrive at a palatial estate, as the sun sets and storm clouds roll in. Each of you, a maid of renown. Each of you, burdened by a tragic past and dark secrets. And each of you, clutching a mysterious invitation from the estate's master: restore the manor, bring revelry back to it, and receive your heart's most secret desire in return.

Only, the master isn't here. For that matter, nobody's lived here as long as you can remember. And as you all get the mansion clean and ready to host the masquerade ball, more and more creepy crawly scary guests arrive at the revelry...and they want you to stay, and entertain them. Forever! Unless you can all somehow fulfill the master's last wish and win back your freedom...
---
Game Host: Saoirse
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ (vulgar humor, sexual references, horror played for comedy)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87745.title: Eyes on the Prize: Catseye Crown
87745.description:
"Eyes on the Prize is a collaborative game of court intrigue and fake dating, in which players assume the role of characters in a couple pretending to be very much in love in order to climb the social ladder of the court for personal gain: be that wealth, status, their families approval, or whatever other motive. Players enact a series of scenes to develop their characters, the relationship -however it turns, and to advance their goals. 

For this session, the backdrop of the drama is the courtiers, some legitimate, some less so, vying to be chosen as the successors to, or position themselves in advantageous relationship to, the hairless Catseye Queen. 

Will your character find wealth, power, friendship, failure, embarrassment, or even true love of one kind or another? Let's find out!
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87754.title: D&D 5e (2024): Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn
87754.description:
"Uni the unicorn is in trouble. The evil spellcaster Kelek has stolen Uni’s horn to power a fell ritual. If the characters don’t stop Kelek, Uni’s horn will be destroyed forever in Kelek’s quest for power.

Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn is part of a yearlong celebration of Dungeons & Dragons and its 50th anniversary. The adventure presented herein involves characters from the beloved 1980s Dungeons & Dragons animated series.

This adventure is designed for four to six level 4 characters. Accompanying the adventure are character sheets for six of the animated series’ protagonists: Bobby, Diana, Eric, Hank, Presto, and Sheila, all presented in young adulthood. The seventh character sheet presents Niko, a Cleric from a different set of adventurers who recently tumbled into the D&D multiverse. Each player can choose one of these characters or provide a character of their own.
---
Game Host: Joseph 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87769.title: Legend of the Five Rings (1st Edition): Lesser of Two Evils
87769.description:
"Your group of magistrates has been summoned from the territories of all the Great Clans, to a remote stronghold on the edge of Crab Clan territory. The locals believe an evil is plaguing the land, and recent tremors have them whispering that the earth spirits are discontent. It is your job to quell their superstitions and get production back on schedule.. No need to worry about the monsters of the Shadowlands way out near the Shinomen Forest, after all. And certainly no chance you will have to do something you regret to avoid a greater catastrophe!

Legend of the Five Rings is a game of samurai drama and danger, focusing on the seven Great Clans of Rokugan and the Clan War that consumes the nation after the death of the Emperor. Characters will be samurai, priests, or monks who work together for the good of the Empire but also try to further the aims of their own Clan.
---
Game Host: Ryan
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

86691.description: What do you get when you cross live theater, improv, and a bunch of cosplaying nerds? You get Cosplay Chess! If you've ever been curious about the event or have never heard of it before today, this is the panel for you! Join our panelists as we discuss how 50+ people get organized into 2 hilarious games of Sakura-Con Cosplay Chess! Learn how the selection process works, how easy it is to apply, and the many different roles required to make Cosplay Chess happen! Get answers to questions like, "How do you choose who makes it into Cosplay Chess? What are Stage Ninjas? Can I be [CHARACTER NAME] next year?" And more!

86967.description: There are so many problems in the world: from famine to war to the discontinuation of All You Can Eat Shrimp at Red Lobster. But there are still so many things that have gone right in the world for us to enjoy… like the live action Death Note film directed by Adam Wingard.

87042.title: Kintsugi Your Life: Using Forgiveness To Mend The Cracks Of The Past
87042.description: Were you bullied or socially shunned for enjoying anime or video games? The pain from these experiences often persists into our adult lives, manifesting as anxiety, self-doubt, and a detachment from our own hearts. Through the healing metaphor of Kintsugi — the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery with gold — you will learn a practical method for releasing past trauma and finding forgiveness for others and yourself. You'll walk away feeling lighter, more empowered, and more at peace with the story that made you who you are.

87722.title: Call of Cthulhu: The Son of Nyx (Nyukusu No Musuko)
87722.description:
"The year is 2098 and you are crew on the spaceship Sakigake conducting a mineral survey on the surface of Pluto. But when you begin your journey back to earth, disaster strikes. A red-black swirling celestial body crashes into Pluto causing untold destruction. The last thing you see before losing consciousness are the eyes of an unspeakable cosmic horror rising from the chaos. Can you escape your doom and return to earth? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided.
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87179.title: Indentifying & Navigating Fabrics without Joanns
87179.description: Fabrics are amazing, and also super confusing. Losing Joanns Fabrics and forcing lots of us to buy online has made it all the more intimidating to start making a cosplay. With this panel, hopefully it will be less intimating, as we cover topics like a basic intro to textiles, discussion on how fabrics change perception, and a discussion on where to buy. Whether you're a new sewist who has a hard time knowing what fabric is which, or your a veteran who isn't sure where to buy online, join this panel and lets mourn the loss of Joanns together.

87274.title: Anime Character Tierlist Roulette
87274.description: Have you ever wondered if your favorite shonen protagonist would be good at running a massive tax evasion scheme? What about how your favorite ship would stack up against the rest in a knitting competition? In this zany panel, we're going to use a wheel to spin for strange arbitrary hypotheticals to rank anime characters by, and we'll all have to live with however the results end up. If you're the kind of person who wonders whether Sakura from Naruto would be a better F1 driver than Hatsune Miku, this might just be the panel for you!

86296.title: Crunchyroll Presents: LIAR GAME Premiere Screening
86296.description: College student, Nao Kanzaki, suddenly receives an odd letter and a suitcase containing 100 million yen. She’s been chosen to participate in the LIAR GAME, a high stakes psychological game where deception and betrayal is necessary. To survive, the painfully honest Nao must team up with former con artist, Shinichi Akiyama to outwit her opponents. Who can she trust? What lies ahead in these twisted games? Join us for the premiere screening of LIAR GAME

85705.title: Seibu Ryu Iai-Battōjutsu - Art of The Japanese Sword
85705.description: Our presentation is a 1-hour demonstration of the Japanese martial art of Iai-Battōjutsu. In the presentation, we will give a history of the art while demonstrating a series of our techniques. We begin with basic draws of the sword and cuts. Next, we move on to kata (forms) demonstration, including a few basic, intermediate, and advanced kata. Finally, the last part of our demonstration is tameshigiri, or “test cutting” of tatami mats with live blades.In the past, we have also performed a choreographed sequenced and performed our tameshigiri at the closing ceremonies of SakuraCon, which we would be happy to do again.

85696.title: Free Build Session
85696.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

87792.title: Pathfinder Society: A Star's Journey [PFS2 7-16]
87792.description:
"A Pathfinder Society adventure for 1st-2nd level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
During the exploration of an ancient Jistkan ruin in Katapesh, the automaton Star goes missing, leaving their archeologist companions worried for their safety. The head archeologist calls upon the Pathfinder Society for assistance, but warns the agents that arrive that the ruin is filled with dangerous creatures and traps and that they should be careful while exploring. A strange frozen automaton meets the Pathfinders on each floor, slowly pointing the way deeper and deeper into the ruin. Will they find Star? Or does the automaton have more malicious motives?
---
Game Host: Nick H
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

87763.title: Break!!RPG: A Completely Normal Adventure
87763.description:
"The peaceful domain of Millbrook faces its darkest hour. A monstrous beast stalks the woods, leaving destruction in its wake. The just Lord Vance grieves for his kidnapped daughter, Princess Helena, taken from her very tower. His scheming Vizier whispers plots in shadowed halls while a mad street prophet warns of apocalyptic doom. Anarchists stir in the taverns, an orphan child claims mythic power, and a traveling merchant dealing in secrets watches it all unfold with knowing eyes. 

You've been called to save the princess, slay the beast, and restore order to this troubled land. It should be simple, every hero knows this story. But as you peel back the layers of Millbrook's tragedy, you'll find things are not as they seem. 

Even monsters, they say, can love.

Expect twists, moral complexity, and the chance to either prevent-or survive-a spectacular disaster.
---
Game Host: Mia
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

86786.title: Japanese Urban Legends 2.0
86786.description: Japanese urban legends are like wasabi: they’ll make your eyes water, leave you a little shaky, and you’ll still come back for more. From eerie phone calls from the dead to bathroom ghosts who love asking awkward questions, these tales have haunted generations. And the best part? A couple of these legends aren’t just stories — you can actually go visit the real-world spots where they’re said to lurk. Pack your courage (and maybe some extra tissues), and let’s dive in.

87181.title: 40 Years of Adventure and Exploration in The Legend of Zelda
87181.description: In 1986, Nintendo released the video game "The Legend of Zelda". It became a big success and the rest is history. This adventurous franchise has shaped gaming over the years with several games having influenced other game developers. Sure, some of those games are considered some of the greatest games ever made. What is certain is that The Legend of Zelda has a legacy that has stood the test of time by being innovative and evolving throughout the ages in video games. To this very day, it still captivates the imagination and continues to capture the hearts of millions.

87805.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
87805.description:
Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

87276.title: Funding your Convention Life!
87276.description: Fund Your Future, Fuel Your Fandom! Love conventions but worried about money—and your parents nagging you to get your act together? Learn how to start early by funding your retirement at 18–21, then focus flexible jobs. Learn how to budget for tickets, travel, and lodging. With smart savings and flexible jobs, you can live your fandom life guilt-free while your future grows quietly in the background.

86583.title: Aged to Perfection
86583.description: Fandom and Cosplay are often seen as “childish” but they’re hobbies that can grow with you! Join us for a discussion of participating in cosplay and fandom as you age.
86583.description_display: Fandom and Cosplay are often seen as “childish” but they’re hobbies that can grow with you! Join us for a discussion of participating in cosplay and fandom as you age.

87141.title: Gender, Genre, and Games in Sports Anime
87141.description: What makes a sports anime a sports anime? Is it the thrill of the competition? The forced team building? The homoerotic subtext? Join us to trace the development of shojo and shonen sports anime tropes/conventions from the 1970s (Ace wo Nerae! and Ashita no Joe) all the way up to the modern day (Birdie Wing and Sk8 the Infinity).

87387.title: Kyoto Animation Arson Attack - 7th Year Memorial Service
87387.description: In July 2019, the studio and publisher Kyoto Animation experienced a horrific and devastating arson attack that resulted in the deaths of 36 young animators, making it one of Japan’s worst massacres in recent history. Because the 1st, 3rd, and 7th year memorial services are of particular importance in Japanese tradition, we feel that the people who lost their lives that day were not given the appropriate opportunity to be mourned by their fans due to the interruption caused by the COVID 19 pandemic.  We, as a local Buddhist temple that is a direct descendant of the 1,200 year old Japanese Shingon lineage, believe that being able to provide fans the opportunity to participate in a 7th year memorial service would be particularly significant. We feel that it is our obligation to provide the opportunity for people to grieve and mourn the lives of the victims and honor their memories. Through the Buddhist memorial service, we will be able to convey to the spirits of the deceased that their lives, works, and legacies are meaningful, and will be cherished. This will also help mourners attain the understanding that their lives are one with the victims of the attack. When a work of art moves someone, it becomes an inseparable part of them, and so we carry its legacy on with us through our own lives. In this way, we are all on a shared voyage, helping those people who have positively impacted our lives continue to live on through us, and we hope to allow fans of the works made by the fallen animators to experience a deeper connection with this understanding.

86541.title: Super Mario Bros Super Panel
86541.description: Mario games have been a pinnacle of consistency for the video game industry. Mario media adaptations have been the same thing, but in the opposite direction. Let’s delve into old Mario media, from the live action movie to the Super Show, and decide once and for all which spaghetti is the most overcooked.

86788.description: Ever had a bad convention experience you or your friends laughed about later? Of course you have. And the longer you go to a convention the more of those stories get gathered. Join us for some of the funniest and most tragic stories of cons gone wrong ever experienced.

85697.title: Free Build Session
85697.description: There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

Whether you’re channeling your favorite pop star, belting out a rock anthem, or grooving to an anime opening theme, the stage is yours to dazzle and entertain. Participants of all ages are welcome to showcase their talent and charisma, with songs suitable for all audiences. Bring your best moves, props, and enthusiasm to impress the crowd and our panel of judges.

87747.title: Wander Witch: A Cozy RPG of Fairies and Witches: A Spoonful of Sunlight, A Saucerful of Stars
87747.description:
"As students and staff if witchery and wizardry at the vibrant Brandysprite Castle, it isn't unusual for you, your classmates, and charges to be sent as envoys to friendly fairy communities. Whether it be to gather resources, catch up on the latest tales, aid villagers, or recruit newcomers, the school trusts you to be clever, creative, and kind as you lend your aid. This time, you are responding to reports of a mysterious dreaming sickness that has taken hold in the nearby village of Gossamer. It's up to you to explore, solve riddles, and forge friendships needed to restore the easy rest of its inhabitants. Will this cozy mystery be best solved through quick wits, an open heart, or a bit of both?
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

86969.title: Cosplay in a Post Joann World
86969.description: With Joann Fabrics gone, a lot of cosplayers are feeling the void in our resource pool. Come join our experts, from cosplayers to costume professionals as we outline resource hunting for the modern cosplayer. From fabric to foam, leather and beads, thermoplastics and more, our panel will give you all the resources to bounce back from losing our favorite crafting store. Now with special prizes!

87370.title: Costume Design Principles for Cosplayers
87370.description: Join a Cosplay Specialist as they bring their theatrical costuming experience to cosplaying to demonstrate how to use costume design principles to research, choose materials, and create a cohesive costume. Whether translating anime and video game character designs to real life or creating original cosplay designs from scratch, costume design principles will help you create grounded and artistic costume interpretations as you craft your cosplays.

87046.title: On Borrowed Wings: Gender, Labor, and Exploitation in Armored Core 6
87046.description: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon has a lot to love in its engaging combat design, spreadsheet-perfect mech optimization, and a distinctly lovable (while being totally faceless) cast of characters. You might know it from “mechsploitation” posts in certain circles of the internet or other riffs on the various voices that have your ear as a player. This panel presents a queer reading of the ways that AC6 uses those voices alongside its broader themes of corporate violence, and presents one possible answer for a pressing post-game question: could estrogen really have saved G5 Iguazu?

87765.title: Nachatte Murder Mystery: A Pseudo Murder Mystery : A Snowed-In Tragedy
87765.description:
"In the remote, snow-covered mountains, a secluded cabin offers warmth and refuge to weary travelers. A place to drink, laugh, and escape the bitter cold. But not all who enter seek shelter. Last night was supposed to be a night of celebration, but when you wake with a pounding headache and a hazy memory, you realize something is terribly wrong. A body lies cold in the living room, and none of you can recall what happened. Was it an accident? A crime of passion? Or something far more sinister? With the storm raging outside and no way to leave, there’s only one way forward; piece together the events of last night before the truth is buried beneath the snow forever.
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."
